RBC.note_on_indian_range: Apollo multicentric Indian study (PMC4062657) and PMC11246370 (Indian reproductive-age women) showed RBC range of 3.68–5.55 million/µL in Indian females, lower than Western references, attributable to nutritional deficiencies and higher prevalence of anaemia in the Indian population. Male values are broadly comparable: 4.3–5.8 million/µL.
RBC.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High RBC may indicate dehydration or polycythaemia. Increase fluid intake — water, nimbu pani, coconut water. Avoid smoking. See a doctor.
RBC.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च RBC निर्जलीकरण या पॉलीसायथीमिया का संकेत हो सकता है। खूब पानी, नींबू पानी, नारियल पानी पिएं। धूम्रपान बंद करें।
RBC.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low RBC usually means anaemia. Include iron-rich foods: spinach (palak), methi, rajma, chana, jaggery (gud), beetroot, pomegranate. Combine with Vitamin C foods (amla, lemon) to boost absorption.
RBC.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम RBC का मतलब आमतौर पर एनीमिया होता है। पालक, मेथी, राजमा, चना, गुड़, चुकंदर और अनार खाएं। आंवला और नींबू के साथ खाने से आयरन का अवशोषण बेहतर होता है।
RBC.source: StatPearls NBK604207: M 4.6–6.2, F 4.2–5.4 million/µL. Indian female range: PMC11246370 (3.68–5.55). Apollo India study PMC4062657. Critical thresholds: CLSI EP28-A3c.

Hb.note_on_indian_range: Apollo multicentric India study (PMC4062657, n=10,665) found 95% reference intervals for Hb: Males 12.3–17.0 g/dL, Females 9.9–14.3 g/dL — notably lower upper limit in females compared to WHO standards (12–16 g/dL). WHO defines anaemia as Hb <13 g/dL (men), <12 g/dL (non-pregnant women), <11 g/dL (pregnant women). The lower Indian female range reflects the high prevalence of nutritional anaemia in India.
Hb.specialist_if_critical_low: Haematologist / Physician (severe anaemia, GI bleed, haemolysis, aplastic anaemia)
Hb.urgency_if_critical_low: within 24 hours (consider transfusion threshold <7 g/dL for symptomatic patients)
Hb.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Most common cause of low Hb in India is iron-deficiency anaemia. Eat iron-rich foods: leafy greens (palak, methi), rajma, chana, lentils, jaggery, fortified cereals, liver (if non-vegetarian). Always combine with Vitamin C (amla, tomato, lemon) to enhance iron absorption. Avoid tea/coffee with meals as tannins inhibit iron absorption.
Hb.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम Hb का सबसे सामान्य कारण भारत में आयरन की कमी है। पालक, मेथी, राजमा, दाल, गुड़, चना और फोर्टिफाइड अनाज खाएं। चाय/कॉफी खाने के साथ न लें।
Hb.source: WHO Haemoglobin Concentrations for Diagnosis of Anaemia 2011. Apollo India multicentric study PMC4062657. StatPearls NBK604207. Critical low <7.0 g/dL: transfusion trigger for most guidelines (AABB 2012).

Hct.note_on_indian_range: Hct closely mirrors Hb trends. Apollo India (PMC4062657) shows Indian males 38–52%, females 34–46% as practical working ranges. Chennai study (PMC3552202) confirmed a downward shift in red cell parameters in Indian population vs. Western references, consistent with dietary iron status.
Hct.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low Hct (low PCV) reflects anaemia. Same dietary advice as low Hb: iron-rich foods with Vitamin C. Avoid excess tea/coffee with meals.
Hct.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम PCV एनीमिया का संकेत है। आयरन युक्त आहार लें और खाने के साथ चाय-कॉफी से परहेज करें।
Hct.source: LOINC 20570-8. StatPearls NBK604207 (M 40–54%, F 36–48%). Apollo India PMC4062657. Chennai Indian study PMC3552202.

MCV.note_on_indian_range: MCV reference range (80–100 fL) is consistent across Indian and Western populations; no significant ethnic shift was found in the Apollo India study. However, a low MCV (<80 fL = microcytosis) is extremely common in India due to the high prevalence of iron deficiency and beta-thalassaemia trait, making it a critically important index in the Indian context.
MCV.specialist_if_critical_high: Haematologist (megaloblastic anaemia: B12/folate deficiency, liver disease, hypothyroidism, myelodysplasia)
MCV.specialist_if_critical_low: Haematologist (severe iron deficiency, thalassaemia, sideroblastic anaemia)
MCV.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low MCV = microcytic red cells; most common cause in India is iron deficiency. Increase dietary iron (palak, dal, rajma, jaggery, sesame). Rule out thalassaemia trait with a HbA2 electrophoresis.
MCV.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High MCV = macrocytic red cells; often due to B12 or folate deficiency. Increase foods rich in Vitamin B12 (milk, curd, paneer, eggs, fish) and folate (leafy greens, dal, sprouts). See a doctor for B12/folate testing.
MCV.source: StatPearls NBK604207: 80–100 fL. NIH NBK260. Apollo India PMC4062657 showed no significant MCV gender difference. Critical thresholds: standard clinical practice (CLSI, labpedia.net).

MCH.note_on_indian_range: MCH parallels MCV closely. Low MCH is very common in Indian labs due to widespread iron deficiency. The reference range of 27–33 pg is consistent with Indian lab practice. However, labs in high-thalassaemia regions (Gujarat, Maharashtra) frequently see MCH 20–25 pg as a common incidental finding in thalassaemia trait carriers.
MCH.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low MCH means red cells are pale (hypochromic) — classic iron deficiency. Eat iron-rich foods: palak, kaddu ke beej (pumpkin seeds), rajma, chana, jaggery. Take Vitamin C with meals.
MCH.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम MCH का मतलब लाल रक्त कोशिकाओं में हीमोग्लोबिन कम है। पालक, राजमा, गुड़ और नींबू का सेवन बढ़ाएं।
MCH.source: StatPearls NBK604207 (27–32 pg). Medscape 2054497 (27–33 pg). NIH NBK260. LOINC 785-6.

MCHC.note_on_indian_range: The Chennai Indian study (PMC3552202) found 64.3% of Indian males and 44% of Indian females had MCHC values outside the existing Western reference ranges, suggesting Indian-specific MCHC ranges need further validation. The lower end of normal in Indian labs may be around 30–31 g/dL in some regions. MCHC >36 g/dL is clinically significant and suggests hereditary spherocytosis, dehydration, or haemoglobin C disease.
MCHC.specialist_if_critical_high: Haematologist (hereditary spherocytosis, intravascular haemolysis, cold agglutinin disease)
MCHC.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low MCHC is often linked to iron deficiency or thalassaemia. Follow an iron-rich diet and consult a doctor for further workup.
MCHC.source: LOINC LP17695-5. StatPearls NBK604207. Medscape 2054497. Chennai Indian study PMC3552202.

RDW.note_on_indian_range: RDW >14.5% (elevated) indicates anisocytosis. In India, high RDW is frequently seen with iron deficiency, mixed nutritional deficiencies (iron + B12/folate), haemoglobinopathies, and post-transfusion states. An elevated RDW combined with low MCV strongly suggests iron deficiency (vs. low MCV with normal RDW which suggests thalassaemia trait). Normal lower threshold (low RDW) is clinically less significant.
RDW.specialist_if_critical_high: Haematologist (severe nutritional anaemia, MDS, haemolytic anaemia workup)
RDW.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High RDW often means multiple nutritional deficiencies. Increase iron (leafy greens, dal, jaggery), Vitamin B12 (milk, paneer, eggs), and folate (green vegetables, sprouts). Consult a doctor for targeted supplementation.
RDW.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च RDW अक्सर कई पोषण की कमियों का संकेत है। पालक, दाल, गुड़, दूध, दही खाएं और डॉक्टर से सलाह लें।
RDW.source: StatPearls NBK604207 (11.5–15%). NIH NBK260 (13 ± 1.5%). Indian context: iron deficiency RDW discrimination — Bessman et al. Am J Clin Pathol 1983;80:322. LOINC 788-0.

Plt.note_on_indian_range: Apollo multicentric India study (PMC4062657, n=10,665) established Indian-specific platelet reference intervals: Males 1.3–3.8 Lakhs/µL (130,000–380,000), Females 1.3–4.2 Lakhs/µL (130,000–420,000). The study noted low platelet counts in Chennai population. Goel et al. 2025 (Wiley IJLH, PMID 39365043) found Indian blood donor platelet range: 115,600–370,000/µL. Dengue, malaria, and typhoid — highly prevalent in India — are major causes of thrombocytopaenia.
Plt.specialist_if_critical_high: Haematologist (essential thrombocythaemia, reactive thrombocytosis — malignancy, infection, iron deficiency)
Plt.specialist_if_critical_low: Haematologist / Physician (dengue, ITP, DIC, aplastic anaemia — urgent if <20,000)
Plt.urgency_if_critical_low: immediate if <20,000 (bleeding risk); within 24 hours if 20,000–50,000
Plt.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low platelets (thrombocytopaenia) in India is often from dengue, malaria, or typhoid — this is a medical emergency. Do NOT try to treat at home. Papaya leaf extract (Carica papaya) has some evidence for dengue-related thrombocytopaenia but is NOT a replacement for hospital care. See a doctor immediately if platelets are below 50,000.
Plt.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम प्लेटलेट्स भारत में अक्सर डेंगू, मलेरिया या टाइफाइड से होती हैं — यह एक चिकित्सकीय आपातस्थिति है। तुरंत डॉक्टर से मिलें।
Plt.source: Apollo India multicentric PMC4062657. Goel et al. IJLH 2025 PMID 39365043. CLSI critical low <50,000/µL. Thrombocytosis critical >1,000,000 (WHO definition of extreme thrombocytosis).

ESR.note_on_indian_range: Apollo India study (PMC4062657) derived ESR reference intervals: Males 2–22 mm/hr, Females 4–55 mm/hr, markedly wider than Western Westergren reference ranges (M: 0–20, F: 0–30). The study noted higher ESR in Chennai population, possibly related to diet, worm infestations, and higher background inflammation. An ESR >100 mm/hr in India warrants urgent investigation for temporal arteritis, multiple myeloma, severe infection, or collagen vascular disease.
ESR.specialist_if_critical_high: Physician / Rheumatologist / Haematologist (PMR, temporal arteritis, myeloma, SLE, severe infection, TB)
ESR.indian_diet_tip_high_en: A very high ESR is not a diagnosis but a signal of inflammation. Maintain a balanced diet rich in anti-inflammatory foods: turmeric (haldi) milk, ginger (adrak), omega-3 rich flaxseeds (alsi). See a doctor for a complete workup.
ESR.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: बहुत अधिक ESR सूजन का संकेत है। हल्दी वाला दूध, अदरक और अलसी का सेवन करें और डॉक्टर से जांच करवाएं।
ESR.source: Apollo multicentric India study PMC4062657. Westergren ESR method LOINC 4537-7. Critical >100 mm/hr: standard clinical threshold (Harrison's Principles 21st ed).

HbA1C.note_on_indian_range: ADA diagnostic criteria (2024) and Diabetes India guidelines use identical HbA1c cut-offs. However, Indian/South Asian populations are known to develop type 2 diabetes at lower BMI and younger age than Western populations. The Research Society for the Study of Diabetes in India (RSSDI) recommends an HbA1c target of ≤7.0% for most diabetic patients in India. HbA1c may be falsely low in haemolytic anaemias, iron deficiency (being treated), and haemoglobin variants (HbS, HbC, HbE) — all of which are common in India. In such cases, fructosamine or alternative glycaemia markers should be used.
HbA1C.specialist_if_critical_high: Endocrinologist / Diabetologist (HbA1c >10% = poor glycaemic control; >13% = crisis level, high DKA or HHS risk)
HbA1C.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High HbA1c = poor blood sugar control over 3 months. Switch to a low-glycaemic Indian diet: prioritise whole grains (jowar, bajra, brown rice), dal, sabzi, curd. Avoid maida (refined flour), refined sugar, sweet drinks, and white rice in large portions. Walk at least 30 minutes daily.
HbA1C.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च HbA1c = 3 महीनों में खराब ब्लड शुगर नियंत्रण। जोवार, बाजरा, ब्राउन राइस, दाल, सब्जी खाएं। मैदा, चीनी और मीठे पेय पदार्थों से परहेज करें। रोज 30 मिनट चलें।
HbA1C.source: ADA Standards of Care 2024 (HbA1c thresholds). RSSDI guidelines India. LOINC 4548-4. CHOP reference ranges document: normal 3.8–5.9%. Haemoglobin variant interference: NGSP.org.

BGL.note_on_indian_range: Apollo India multicentric study (PMC4062657) found fasting blood glucose 95% reference interval in males: 78–110 mg/dL — slightly wider than ADA standard (70–99 mg/dL). India has the world's second highest number of diabetic patients (>100 million). ICMR and RSSDI recommend fasting glucose ≥126 mg/dL (on 2 occasions) for diabetes diagnosis. 100–125 mg/dL = IFG (Impaired Fasting Glucose = prediabetes). Indian labs commonly use the term 'FBS' (fasting blood sugar).
BGL.specialist_if_critical_low: Physician / Emergency (severe hypoglycaemia: confusion, seizure, loss of consciousness)
BGL.urgency_if_critical_high: immediate if >400 with symptoms (HHS/DKA); within 24 hrs if 300–400
BGL.urgency_if_critical_low: immediate (hypoglycaemic emergency — give glucose or sugar water)
BGL.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High fasting glucose: eat low-glycaemic foods — jowar roti, mixed dal, vegetables, curd. Avoid white rice in large portions, maida products, fruit juice, packaged sweets. Exercise daily. Monitor regularly.
BGL.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च उपवास ग्लूकोज: ज्वार रोटी, मिश्रित दाल, सब्जियां, दही खाएं। सफेद चावल, मैदा, फलों का रस और मिठाइयों से परहेज करें।
BGL.source: ADA Standards 2024. RSSDI India guidelines. Apollo India PMC4062657 (FBG 78–110 mg/dL in Indian males). Critical low <50 mg/dL (symptomatic neuroglycopaenia — Whipple's triad threshold). Critical high >500 mg/dL (HHS risk).

TSH.note_on_indian_range: The Indian reference range for TSH is broadly consistent with international ranges (0.4–4.0 mIU/L). However, iodine intake variability across India (coastal vs. inland populations) can affect the background TSH distribution. The Thyroid Research Society of India (TRSI) and ATA recommend identical diagnostic thresholds. TSH >10 mIU/L in the absence of symptoms = subclinical hypothyroidism warranting treatment consideration. Overt hypothyroidism affects approximately 10.95% of Indian population (Unnikrishnan et al., Indian J Endocrinol Metab 2013). Pregnancy-specific lower thresholds are critical as untreated hypothyroidism in pregnancy causes foetal neurocognitive harm.
TSH.specialist_if_critical_high: Endocrinologist (overt hypothyroidism, autoimmune thyroiditis, TSH >20 = urgent thyroid hormone replacement)
TSH.specialist_if_critical_low: Endocrinologist (hyperthyroidism, Graves' disease, toxic nodular goitre; cardiac risk — atrial fibrillation)
TSH.urgency_if_critical_high: within 48 hours (routine); immediate if myxoedema coma suspected
TSH.urgency_if_critical_low: within 24 hours (thyroid storm risk if <0.01 with symptoms)
TSH.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High TSH = hypothyroidism. Avoid cruciferous vegetables (cauliflower, cabbage, broccoli, radish) in large raw amounts as they contain goitrogens. Iodised salt is essential. Soy products in excess can interfere with thyroid hormone absorption. Take thyroid medication (if prescribed) on an empty stomach, 30–60 min before food.
TSH.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च TSH = हाइपोथायरायडिज्म। आयोडाइज्ड नमक का उपयोग करें। अत्यधिक कच्ची पत्तागोभी, मूली से बचें। थायरॉइड दवा खाली पेट लें।
TSH.source: LOINC 3016-3. ATA/AACE 2012 thyroid guidelines. Lewandowski 2015 PMC4480274 (TSH upper limit 4.2–4.5 mIU/L; elderly up to 6 mIU/L). Indian prevalence: Unnikrishnan et al. 2013. Pregnancy-specific thresholds: ATA 2017 guidelines.

ALT.note_on_indian_range: Apollo India multicentric study (PMC4062657) found higher ALT levels in Delhi and Hyderabad centres than national average. Indian labs commonly use 0–40 U/L (males) and 0–35 U/L (females) as their local references. Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) is highly prevalent in India (~9–32%), making ALT one of the most commonly abnormal liver enzymes. A common clinical cut-off used in India: ALT >3× ULN = significant hepatocellular injury warranting workup. Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C, and NASH are the main causes of persistently elevated ALT in India.
ALT.specialist_if_critical_high: Hepatologist / Gastroenterologist (acute hepatitis, NASH, drug-induced liver injury, Wilson's disease)
ALT.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24–48 hours if ALT >500 U/L; immediate if ALT >1000 U/L (acute hepatic injury)
ALT.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High ALT = liver stress. Avoid alcohol completely. Reduce oily, fried, processed foods. Eat a simple diet: khichdi, dal, sabzi, curd. Avoid paracetamol overuse. Maintain a healthy weight. Consult a gastroenterologist.
ALT.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च ALT = लिवर पर दबाव। शराब पूरी तरह बंद करें। तला-भुना खाना कम खाएं। खिचड़ी, दाल, सब्जी खाएं। पेरासिटामोल का अत्यधिक सेवन न करें।
ALT.source: LOINC 1742-6. Labcorp/ARUP reference M 7–56, F 7–45 U/L. Apollo India PMC4062657. NASH India prevalence: Duseja 2010 J Clin Exp Hepatol. AASLD ALT >3× ULN threshold.

ALP.note_on_indian_range: Indian standard adult range used by most labs: 40–150 U/L. ALP is elevated in growing children (bone growth), pregnancy (placental isoenzyme), and liver disease or biliary obstruction. In India, ALP elevation is commonly caused by biliary obstruction (choledocholithiasis — common in North India), hepatitis, and bone disease (vitamin D deficiency, Paget's disease). Vitamin D deficiency is highly prevalent in India (>70–80% in some studies), causing secondary hyperparathyroidism and mildly elevated ALP.
ALP.specialist_if_critical_high: Gastroenterologist / Hepatologist for biliary disease (>3× ULN); Endocrinologist for bone disease
ALP.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High ALP in liver disease context: avoid alcohol, fatty foods. For bone-related high ALP, ensure adequate Vitamin D (sunlight 15–20 min/day) and calcium (milk, curd, ragi). Consult a doctor for specific cause.
ALP.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च ALP: शराब बंद करें। विटामिन D के लिए धूप लें और रागी, दूध, दही खाएं। डॉक्टर से कारण जानें।
ALP.source: LOINC 6768-6. ARUP Labs reference 44–147 U/L. Furruqh et al. Indian J Clin Biochem 2004 (urban South Indian ALP norms). Vitamin D deficiency India: Ritu & Gupta, Eur J Clin Nutr 2014.

INR.note_on_indian_range: INR reference range of 0.8–1.2 is universally applicable. In India, warfarin and anticoagulation monitoring is common in patients with rheumatic heart disease (high prevalence in India), atrial fibrillation, and mechanical heart valves. Many Indian patients have subtherapeutic or supratherapeutic INR due to dietary inconsistency (variable Vitamin K intake from green vegetables) and self-medication. An INR >5.0 carries significant spontaneous bleeding risk; >8.0 is a haematological emergency.
INR.specialist_if_critical_high: Haematologist / Emergency Physician (INR >5.0 = high bleed risk; Vitamin K or FFP reversal may be needed)
INR.specialist_if_critical_low: Physician (INR <0.5 may indicate a hypercoagulable state, though rarely clinically critical alone)
INR.urgency_if_critical_high: immediate if INR >8.0 or active bleeding; within 24 hours if INR 5–8
INR.indian_diet_tip_high_en: If on warfarin and INR is too high: avoid sudden changes in green leafy vegetable intake (palak, methi, hara pyaz). Consistent daily intake is better than elimination. Avoid alcohol. Report to your doctor immediately if any unusual bleeding.
INR.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: वार्फरिन पर हों तो हरी पत्तेदार सब्जियों का सेवन अचानक न बदलें। शराब से बचें। असामान्य रक्तस्राव होने पर तुरंत डॉक्टर को बताएं।
INR.source: LOINC 34714-6. CLSI critical INR >5.0 (spontaneous bleed risk). ACCP anticoagulation guidelines 2012. Indian context: rheumatic heart disease prevalence India — RHD-India registry.

APTT.note_on_indian_range: APTT reference range is reagent-dependent; Indian labs typically use 25–40 seconds for their local reference. The most common causes of prolonged APTT in India are: heparin therapy (monitored therapeutic anticoagulation), haemophilia A/B (factor VIII/IX deficiency — estimated 1 in 5,000–10,000 in India), lupus anticoagulant (antiphospholipid syndrome), and DIC (disseminated intravascular coagulation from sepsis, obstetric emergencies). APTT >2.5× normal without heparin suggests significant coagulopathy.
APTT.specialist_if_critical_high: Haematologist (haemophilia, lupus anticoagulant, DIC); Emergency Physician (DIC, heparin overdose)
APTT.urgency_if_critical_high: immediate if active bleeding with APTT >100 seconds
APTT.urgency_if_watch: within 48 hours if isolated mild elevation without bleeding
APTT.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Prolonged APTT needs medical workup — it is not manageable by diet alone. If on heparin, ensure your doses are monitored. Report any unusual bruising or bleeding immediately.
APTT.source: LOINC 3173-2. Standard lab reference 25–40 sec. Heparin monitoring therapeutic range 60–100 sec (ACCP). Haemophilia India prevalence: Haemophilia Federation India 2019.

Na.note_on_indian_range: The Indian sodium reference range is identical to international standards (136–145 mEq/L). However, India has specific clinical contexts: hyponatraemia is extremely common in Indian patients with diarrhoea/vomiting (especially in children), cirrhosis (very prevalent), SIADH from CNS infections (TB meningitis — endemic in India), and excessive IV saline administration. Hypernatraemia occurs in dehydration, common in elderly patients. The Apollo India study confirmed standard electrolyte ranges apply to the Indian population.
Na.specialist_if_critical_high: Physician / Intensivist (severe hypernatraemia — cerebral dehydration risk; neurological symptoms at >160 mEq/L)
Na.specialist_if_critical_low: Physician / Intensivist (severe hyponatraemia — cerebral oedema, seizure risk at <120 mEq/L; rapid correction risk of osmotic demyelination)
Na.urgency_if_critical_low: immediate (symptomatic hyponatraemia <120 mEq/L or acute onset)
Na.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High sodium: reduce table salt, pickles (achar), papads, processed foods, canned foods. Switch to low-sodium salts if advised. Increase water intake unless restricted by a doctor.
Na.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च सोडियम: नमक, अचार, पापड़ और पैकेट बंद खाना कम खाएं। डॉक्टर की सलाह से पानी की मात्रा बढ़ाएं।
Na.source: LOINC 2951-2. Standard reference 136–145 mEq/L (Harrison's 21st ed). CLSI critical low <120, critical high >160 mEq/L. Apollo India PMC4062657. India-specific context: TB meningitis SIADH — Rizvi et al.

K.note_on_indian_range: Standard potassium range (3.5–5.1 mEq/L) is applicable across populations including India. BPPH (biorXiv / HCUP LOINC code 6298-4 maps to same analyte). Hypokalaemia is very common in India due to: diarrhoea/vomiting, diuretic use, amphotericin B therapy (antifungal — used for kala-azar/leishmaniasis in India), hypomagnesaemia. Hyperkalaemia is seen in acute kidney injury, CKD, ACE inhibitor/ARB use (common in Indian hypertensive patients), and adrenal insufficiency. Pseudohyperkalaemia (haemolysed sample) is a very frequent artefact in Indian labs — always repeat before acting.
K.specialist_if_critical_high: Physician / Cardiologist / Intensivist (hyperkalaemia >6.5: cardiac arrhythmia — wide QRS, peaked T waves; >7.0 = cardiac arrest risk — treat with calcium gluconate immediately)
K.specialist_if_critical_low: Physician (hypokalaemia <2.5: ventricular arrhythmia risk, muscle weakness, paralysis)
K.urgency_if_critical_low: immediate if symptomatic (<2.5 mEq/L or arrhythmia)
K.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High potassium (hyperkalaemia): reduce high-potassium foods: banana, coconut water, orange juice, potato, tomato, spinach, dal (excessive). Follow a renal diet if CKD is the cause.
K.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low potassium (hypokalaemia): eat potassium-rich foods: banana, coconut water, sweet potato, tomato, dal, green leafy vegetables. Ensure adequate hydration.
K.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम पोटेशियम: केला, नारियल पानी, शकरकंद, दाल और हरी सब्जियां खाएं।
K.source: LOINC 2823-3 (LOINC 6298-4 also used). Reference 3.5–5.1 mEq/L (Harrison's 21st ed). CLSI critical low <2.5, critical high >6.5 mEq/L. India kala-azar amphotericin B hypokalaemia: WHO KA treatment guidelines.

LDL.note_on_indian_range: Apollo India multicentric study (PMC4062657) found 95% reference interval for LDL in Indian males: 60–176 mg/dL, Indian females: 61–175 mg/dL — broadly consistent with ATP-III desirable levels but with wide upper limits due to population variance. South Indian lipid study (Malati & Mahesh, Indian J Clin Biochem 2009) confirmed similar ranges. Indian/South Asian patients have a specific lipid profile with small, dense LDL particles (atherogenic, even at lower total LDL levels) and low HDL, making cardiovascular risk higher at a given LDL level compared to Western populations. Many Indian cardiologists target LDL <70 mg/dL for secondary prevention.
LDL.specialist_if_critical_high: Cardiologist / Lipidologist (LDL >190 = familial hypercholesterolaemia screen; statin therapy indicated)
LDL.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High LDL: reduce saturated fats (ghee, dalda, full-fat dairy, processed foods, biscuits). Increase fibre: oats, isabgol (psyllium husk), whole pulses, fruits, vegetables. Replace palm oil/coconut oil with mustard oil or olive oil in moderation. Exercise regularly.
LDL.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च LDL: घी, डालडा और तला हुआ खाना कम करें। ओट्स, इसबगोल, दालें और सब्जियां बढ़ाएं। व्यायाम करें।
LDL.source: LOINC 2089-1. ATP-III (NCEP 2002) classification. ACC/AHA 2019 guidelines (LDL <70 mg/dL secondary prevention). Apollo India PMC4062657. South Indian lipid study: Malati & Mahesh IJCB 2009.

HDL.note_on_indian_range: Indian population studies consistently show lower HDL levels than Western populations. Apollo India study (PMC4062657) 95% reference intervals: Males 31–69 mg/dL, Females 26–77 mg/dL. South Indian lipid study (Malati & Mahesh 2009): HDL Male 26–57, Female 26–59 mg/dL. Indian/South Asian patients are particularly prone to low HDL due to genetic predisposition, high refined carbohydrate intake, physical inactivity, and metabolic syndrome. HDL <40 mg/dL (men) or <50 mg/dL (women) is a component of metabolic syndrome — highly prevalent in urban India.
HDL.specialist_if_critical_low: Cardiologist / Lipidologist (very low HDL <20 mg/dL = very high CVD risk; rule out hypertriglyceridaemia, genetic HDL deficiency)
HDL.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low HDL: increase physical activity (the most effective way to raise HDL). Include omega-3 fatty acids: flaxseeds (alsi), walnuts, fatty fish. Reduce refined carbohydrates and trans fats. Moderate alcohol (if socioculturally acceptable) can raise HDL, but this is not recommended as a therapeutic strategy.
HDL.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम HDL: नियमित व्यायाम करें। अलसी, अखरोट, सरसों का तेल खाएं। रिफाइंड कार्बोहाइड्रेट कम करें।
HDL.source: LOINC 2085-9. ATP-III HDL classification. Apollo India PMC4062657. South Indian lipid study Malati & Mahesh 2009. Indian metabolic syndrome prevalence: Gupta et al. J Assoc Physicians India 2004.

eGFR.note_on_indian_range: eGFR is calculated from serum creatinine using CKD-EPI 2021 (creatinine ± cystatin C) equation. India has >150 million patients with CKD (Jha et al. Lancet 2013), with diabetic nephropathy and hypertensive nephrosclerosis as the top causes. The MDRD equation (older) slightly underestimates GFR in South Asian populations at higher GFR levels; CKD-EPI 2021 is now preferred. eGFR should be interpreted alongside urine ACR (albumin:creatinine ratio). Kidney failure (G5, eGFR <15) requires nephrology referral for dialysis planning in India.
eGFR.specialist_if_critical_low: Nephrologist (eGFR <30 = specialist referral mandatory in most guidelines; <15 = prepare for dialysis/transplant)
eGFR.urgency_if_critical_low: within 24–48 hours if eGFR acutely drops to <30; within 1 week if chronic and stable
eGFR.urgency_if_watch: within 2–4 weeks (monitor trend over 3 months to confirm CKD staging)
eGFR.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low eGFR (CKD): reduce dietary protein (avoid excessive meat, dal in advanced CKD), reduce salt (<5g/day), restrict potassium and phosphorus intake based on stage. Avoid NSAIDs (ibuprofen, diclofenac — commonly overused in India). Stay well hydrated unless restricted. Follow a renal dietitian's advice.
eGFR.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम eGFR (CKD): प्रोटीन, नमक, पोटेशियम और फास्फोरस सीमित करें। NSAID दवाएं न लें। नेफ्रोलॉजिस्ट से मिलें।
eGFR.source: LOINC 62238-1. KDIGO 2012 CKD classification (G1–G5). CKD-EPI 2021 equation (NEJM 2021;385:1737). India CKD burden: Jha et al. Lancet 2013;382:260–272.

PSA.note_on_indian_range: PSA cut-off of 4.0 ng/mL is standard in India and internationally. Levels 4.0–10 ng/mL = 'grey zone' (25% prostate cancer risk); >10 ng/mL = high suspicion (>50% prostate cancer risk). Free-to-total PSA ratio <15% in the grey zone increases cancer probability. Prostate cancer is the 3rd most common cancer in Indian males. PSA velocity (rate of rise >0.75 ng/mL/year) is also clinically significant. BPH (benign prostatic hyperplasia) and prostatitis also elevate PSA; a rectal examination and urine culture are required for full interpretation.
PSA.specialist_if_critical_high: Urologist (PSA >10 ng/mL or rising PSA velocity — prostate biopsy evaluation)
PSA.urgency_if_critical_high: within 2–4 weeks (non-emergency but urgent urology referral)
PSA.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Elevated PSA warrants medical workup — diet alone cannot treat it. For prostate health, a diet rich in tomatoes (lycopene — antioxidant), green tea, pomegranate, and low in red meat/dairy fat is often recommended, though evidence is observational. Consult a urologist.
PSA.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च PSA: टमाटर, हरी चाय, अनार खाएं। लाल मांस और फुल-फैट डेयरी कम करें। यूरोलॉजिस्ट से मिलें।
PSA.source: LOINC 2857-1. StatPearls NBK557495. Age-specific PSA cut-offs: Carter et al. JAMA 1992. Free PSA ratio: Catalona et al. NEJM 1998. India prostate cancer: ICMR 2021 cancer registry.

AFP.note_on_indian_range: AFP reference cut-off for adults: <10 ng/mL (most Indian labs use this standard). AFP is the primary tumour marker for: (1) Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) — India has a significant HCC burden linked to HBV and HCV infections; AFP >400 ng/mL with liver mass on imaging is highly suggestive of HCC. (2) Non-seminomatous testicular germ cell tumours (NSGCTs). (3) Prenatal neural tube defect and trisomy 18/21 screening (maternal serum AFP). In India, HBV-related HCC is common; AFP surveillance every 6 months is recommended for cirrhotic patients. AFP can also be mildly elevated in acute viral hepatitis, cirrhosis, and inflammatory bowel disease — this does not necessarily indicate malignancy.
AFP.specialist_if_critical_high: Hepatologist / Oncologist (AFP >400 ng/mL with liver lesion = HCC workup urgently; Oncologist if testicular tumour suspected)
AFP.urgency_if_critical_high: within 48–72 hours (urgent imaging and specialist referral)
AFP.urgency_if_watch: within 1–2 weeks (mild elevation 10–40 ng/mL in known hepatitis/cirrhosis — trend monitoring)
AFP.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Elevated AFP requires immediate medical evaluation — it is a tumour marker. For liver health: avoid alcohol, limit fatty foods, get vaccinated for Hepatitis B (if not already done). Report to a gastroenterologist or oncologist.
AFP.source: LOINC 1834-1. Regenstrief Institute (LOINC LP14331-0). AFP cut-off: AASLD HCC guidelines 2018. AFP >400 ng/mL sensitivity for HCC: Trevisani et al. J Hepatol 2001. India HCC and HBV: Anand et al. J Gastroenterol Hepatol 2008.

BNP.note_on_indian_range: NT-proBNP reference cut-offs are internationally standardised and applicable to Indian patients. ESC (2021) guidelines recommend BNP <35 pg/mL or NT-proBNP <125 pg/mL to rule out HF in outpatients. Cardiac failure and RHD (rheumatic heart disease) are major contributors to elevated BNP in India. NT-proBNP levels are higher in women, the elderly, and patients with CKD (common in India). Very high NT-proBNP (>5,000 pg/mL) correlates with poor prognosis in acute decompensated heart failure. In India, it's important to note that NT-proBNP is also elevated in pulmonary hypertension and cor pulmonale (RV failure from lung disease), which are common in India.
BNP.specialist_if_critical_high: Cardiologist (NT-proBNP >5,000 = severe heart failure; urgent echocardiogram and hospitalisation)
BNP.urgency_if_critical_high: immediate if acute dyspnoea (possible acute decompensated HF)
BNP.urgency_if_watch: within 1 week for stable patients in monitoring
BNP.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High NT-proBNP = heart is under strain. Strictly restrict salt (<2g/day), limit fluid intake as advised, avoid alcohol, and take medications as prescribed. Do NOT stop medications without consulting your cardiologist. Report sudden weight gain (>2 kg in 2 days) or worsening breathlessness immediately.
BNP.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च NT-proBNP = हृदय पर दबाव। नमक <2g/दिन रखें, तरल पदार्थ सीमित करें, शराब न पिएं। हृदय रोग विशेषज्ञ की सलाह के बिना दवाएं बंद न करें।
BNP.source: LOINC 33762-6. ESC Heart Failure Guidelines 2021 (NT-proBNP <125 pg/mL rule-out). Medscape 2087425. Generation Scotland NT-proBNP study (Circulation HF 2022). Age-stratified cut-offs: ESC 2021.

pO2.note_on_indian_range: Arterial pO2 of 80–100 mmHg is the standard reference range on room air at sea level. In India, altitude effects are relevant: populations in high-altitude regions (Ladakh, Sikkim) have adapted with lower expected pO2 values (e.g., in Leh at 3,500m, normal pO2 may be 55–65 mmHg). pO2 <60 mmHg = Type 1 Respiratory Failure (hypoxaemic), requiring supplemental oxygen. Common causes in India: pneumonia, COVID-19, TB (major cause of respiratory failure in India), COPD exacerbation. The PaO2/FiO2 ratio <300 defines ALI, <200 defines ARDS.
pO2.specialist_if_critical_low: Pulmonologist / Intensivist (pO2 <60 mmHg = immediate oxygen supplementation; <40 mmHg = ICU / mechanical ventilation consideration)
pO2.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low pO2 is a medical emergency — it means insufficient oxygen reaching blood. This requires immediate supplemental oxygen and medical attention. Deep breathing exercises (pranayama — anulom vilom) help in mild chronic conditions under medical supervision.
pO2.source: LOINC 2703-7. StatPearls NBK536919. ACLS Certification Association ABG reference. Critical pO2 <60: WHO respiratory failure definition. High-altitude India: Nair et al. JAPI 2008.

pCO2.note_on_indian_range: pCO2 reference range 35–45 mmHg is universally applicable. A rising pCO2 (hypercapnia) indicates impaired ventilation. Type 2 respiratory failure (hypercapnic) = pCO2 >45 with pO2 <60. In India, the most common cause is COPD (from tobacco smoking and biomass fuel exposure — highly prevalent in rural India), severe asthma, neuromuscular disease (Guillain-Barré, common post-viral), and chest wall deformity. A pCO2 >55 mmHg in a non-COPD patient, or acutely worsening pCO2 in a known COPD patient, is an indication for NIV (BiPAP/CPAP) or intubation.
pCO2.specialist_if_critical_high: Pulmonologist / Intensivist (pCO2 >70 = severe ventilatory failure — immediate NIV or intubation)
pCO2.specialist_if_critical_low: Physician / Intensivist (pCO2 <20 = severe hyperventilation — anxiety, pulmonary embolism, metabolic acidosis compensation)
pCO2.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Elevated pCO2 means the lungs are not removing CO2 properly — this is a medical emergency. If a COPD patient, ensure regular use of inhalers, avoid smoking completely, and avoid biomass cooking smoke exposure.
pCO2.source: LOINC 2019-8. StatPearls NBK551648. ABG normal ranges: Wikipedia/clinical standards. COPD India prevalence: Jindal et al. Indian J Chest Dis Allied Sci 2006.

SPO2.note_on_indian_range: SpO2 ≥95% on room air is normal for all populations including Indian. SpO2 became a critical vital sign during the COVID-19 pandemic in India (2020–2022) — 'happy hypoxaemia' (SpO2 dropping silently to <90% before symptoms) was a key feature of COVID-19 in Indian patients. Pulse oximeters may be less accurate in patients with dark skin pigmentation, nail polish, anaemia, hypothermia, or poor peripheral perfusion. A persistent SpO2 <94% requires supplemental oxygen; <90% = emergency oxygen (15 L/min non-rebreather mask while arranging hospital care). COPD patients may have a target SpO2 of 88–92% to avoid hypercapnic drive suppression.
SPO2.specialist_if_critical_low: Emergency / Pulmonologist (SpO2 <90% = immediate supplemental oxygen; ICU for <85%)
SPO2.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low SpO2 is a medical emergency. Position the patient sitting upright, provide supplemental oxygen immediately if available (use of portable home oxygen concentrators is common in India). Call for emergency help. Do not delay transport to a hospital.
SPO2.source: LOINC 59408-5. ILD Collaborative (SpO2 normal ≥95%). COVID-19 India 'happy hypoxia': Dhont et al. Respir Res 2020. COPD target SpO2: BTS guidelines 2017. Pulse oximetry skin pigmentation bias: Sjoding et al. NEJM 2020.

FEV1.note_on_indian_range: FEV1 is expressed as a percentage of the predicted value based on age, sex, and height. Indian-specific spirometry prediction equations exist (Indian norms by Kamat et al. 1977, Udwadia et al., and more recently Bhatt et al. Chest 2011) and Indian values are 10–20% lower than NHANES III (Western) predicted values — using Western equations overestimates obstruction in Indian patients. GOLD guidelines recommend using local ethnic norms when available. FEV1/FVC <0.7 (post-bronchodilator) = obstructive pattern (COPD/asthma). COPD is the 4th leading cause of death in India.
FEV1.specialist_if_critical_low: Pulmonologist (FEV1 <50% predicted = moderate-severe COPD; <30% = very severe, specialist management required)
FEV1.urgency_if_critical_low: within 1–2 weeks (scheduled; immediate only if acute exacerbation)
FEV1.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low FEV1 from COPD: stop smoking completely. Avoid biomass fuel smoke (chulha cooking). Use prescribed inhalers correctly. Pulmonary rehabilitation exercises (pursed-lip breathing, incentive spirometry) improve lung capacity. Maintain a healthy BMI — obesity worsens COPD.
FEV1.source: LOINC 20150-9. GOLD 2024 guidelines (FEV1 staging). Indian spirometry norms: Bhatt et al. Chest 2011. Kamat et al. 1977. COPD India burden: Salvi et al. Lancet 2011.

FVC.note_on_indian_range: FVC values in Indian adults are typically 10–20% lower than Western predicted values (same Indian prediction equation caveat as FEV1). A low FVC with a normal FEV1/FVC ratio = restrictive pattern (interstitial lung disease, pleural effusion, neuromuscular disease, obesity). TB-related lung destruction is a major cause of restrictive lung disease in India. Silicosis (from stone quarrying — highly prevalent in states like Rajasthan, Jharkhand) is another important cause. FVC <50% = severe restriction requiring specialist evaluation and possible ventilatory support.
FVC.specialist_if_critical_low: Pulmonologist (severe restrictive lung disease; rule out ILD, silicosis, pleural disease)
FVC.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low FVC indicates stiff or restricted lungs. Pulmonary exercises, respiratory physiotherapy, and treatment of the underlying cause are essential. Maintain adequate nutrition — malnutrition weakens respiratory muscles (common in India).
FVC.source: LOINC 19868-9. ATS/ERS spirometry guidelines 2022. Indian spirometry norms: Bhatt et al. Chest 2011. TB restrictive lung disease India: WHO TB report 2022. Silicosis India: ICMR.

CK.note_on_indian_range: Standard CK reference ranges (males 39–308, females 26–192 U/L) are applicable to South Asian/Indian populations. Asian males have upper reference limits of 227–440 U/L in systematic review data (Aleksovska et al. Eur J Neurol 2025). CK is elevated after muscle injury, intramuscular injections (very common in Indian clinical practice), strenuous exercise, hypothyroidism, myocardial infarction (CK-MB subfraction), and rhabdomyolysis. In India, rhabdomyolysis occurs from heat stroke (common in summer), statin myopathy (statin use is high in urban India), snake envenomation, trauma, and alcohol-related myopathy. CK >1,000 U/L with myoglobinuria = risk of acute kidney injury.
CK.specialist_if_critical_high: Physician / Neurologist (CK >1,000 U/L = rhabdomyolysis workup, myopathy investigation; Cardiologist if CK-MB elevated = AMI)
CK.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24 hours (check renal function; if CK >5,000 with symptoms = immediate IV fluids, hospital admission)
CK.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High CK from muscle injury: rest the affected muscles, stay well hydrated (IV fluids in hospital if very high). If on statins, report to your doctor immediately for statin-induced myopathy assessment. Avoid alcohol.
CK.source: LOINC 2157-6. Aleksovska et al. Eur J Neurol 2025 (PMC12163646) — Asian male upper limit 227–440 U/L. PMC5370787 (NHANES CK reference ranges). Critical >1,000 U/L: AKI rhabdomyolysis threshold (Harrison's 21st ed).

GCS.note_on_indian_range: GCS is a clinical scoring system, not a laboratory test — no Indian-specific adjustment needed. Minimum score = 3 (deep coma/death-like); maximum = 15 (fully alert). Components: Eye Opening (1–4) + Verbal Response (1–5) + Motor Response (1–6) = Total 3–15. GCS ≤8 = indication for airway protection (intubation in many protocols) — the '8 is great, intubate' rule. In India, road traffic accidents (RTAs) are a leading cause of head injury with GCS assessment; poisoning and cerebrovascular accidents (CVA) are also major contributors.
GCS.specialist_if_critical_low: Neurosurgeon / Intensivist (GCS ≤8 = emergency; immediate CT head, airway management, ICU admission)
GCS.urgency_if_watch: within 1–2 hours for GCS 9–12 with declining trend
GCS.indian_diet_tip_low_en: A low GCS is a neurological emergency — nutrition and diet advice are irrelevant acutely. Emergency services (108 in India) should be called immediately for any unconscious patient.
GCS.source: LOINC 35088-4. Teasdale & Jennett, Lancet 1974 (original GCS paper). TBI severity classification: Brain Trauma Foundation 2016. GCS ≤8 intubation guideline: Advanced Trauma Life Support (ATLS) 10th ed. India RTA burden: WHO Global Status Report Road Safety 2023.

IOP.note_on_indian_range: Normal IOP of 10–21 mmHg is universally applicable. India has a high prevalence of glaucoma — estimated 12 million with glaucoma and 1.5 million blind from it (India has the 2nd highest glaucoma burden globally). Most common is primary open-angle glaucoma (POAG) — often asymptomatic until advanced. Acute angle-closure glaucoma (IOP can suddenly spike to 40–70 mmHg) is a medical emergency with severe pain, red eye, and vomiting. IOP may be falsely low after LASIK surgery due to corneal thinning. A low IOP (<5 mmHg = ocular hypotony) can occur after penetrating eye injury or post-surgery.
IOP.specialist_if_critical_high: Ophthalmologist (IOP ≥30 mmHg = urgent glaucoma assessment; acute angle-closure = ophthalmic emergency — IV acetazolamide + pilocarpine eye drops immediately)
IOP.specialist_if_critical_low: Ophthalmologist (IOP <5 = ocular hypotony — risk of visual loss and globe collapse)
IOP.urgency_if_critical_high: immediate for acute angle-closure (IOP >40 with pain, nausea); within 24 hours for chronic elevated IOP >30
IOP.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Elevated IOP: use prescribed eye drops as directed (do not skip). Avoid caffeine excess. Regular moderate exercise is beneficial. Avoid inverted yoga poses (shirshasana — headstand) which acutely increase IOP. Annual eye pressure checks are recommended for all Indians over 40.
IOP.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च IOP: निर्धारित आई ड्रॉप्स नियमित रूप से डालें। शीर्षासन जैसे उल्टे आसन से बचें। 40 वर्ष के बाद सालाना आंखों की जांच करवाएं।
IOP.source: LOINC 56844-4. AAO (American Academy of Ophthalmology) IOP reference 10–21 mmHg. Indian glaucoma burden: ICARE foundation, India Glaucoma Society 2022. ICP normal and ocular hypotony: Friedman et al. Am J Ophthalmol 2009.

BP.note_on_indian_range: India has an estimated 220 million hypertensive patients (one of the highest burdens globally). The ACC/AHA 2017 guidelines (Stage 1 HTN ≥130/80) and JNC7 older cut-off (≥140/90) are both used in India depending on the institution. The ICMR recommends treating BP ≥140/90 mmHg as hypertension. Low BP (hypotension) is clinically significant if symptomatic: systolic <90 mmHg in adults = hypotension; septic shock < 90 mmHg systolic despite fluid resuscitation. Indian patients have a higher risk of stroke at any given BP level than Western populations (Stroke Federation India data).
BP.specialist_if_critical_high: Emergency Physician / Cardiologist (BP ≥180/120 = hypertensive crisis; check for end-organ damage: chest pain, headache, visual changes, confusion)
BP.specialist_if_critical_low: Emergency Physician (systolic <70 = cardiogenic or septic shock — immediate ICU)
BP.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High BP: adopt DASH-like Indian diet — reduce salt (<5g/day; avoid pickles/achar/papads), increase potassium-rich foods (banana, coconut water, tomato), eat more fruits and vegetables, reduce ghee and oil, exercise 30 min/day, lose weight if overweight, avoid alcohol and tobacco.
BP.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च BP: नमक <5g/दिन रखें। अचार, पापड़ से बचें। केला, नारियल पानी, टमाटर खाएं। रोज 30 मिनट व्यायाम करें। शराब और तंबाकू बंद करें।
BP.source: LOINC 55284-4 (systolic 8480-6, diastolic 8462-4). ACC/AHA 2017 BP guidelines. JNC-8 2014. India hypertension burden: Gupta et al. J Hum Hypertens 2019. Critical values: Harrison's 21st ed.

HR.note_on_indian_range: Heart rate reference range (60–100 bpm) is universally applicable. Physiological bradycardia (<60 bpm) is common in trained athletes — not pathological. Pathological bradycardia: complete heart block, sick sinus syndrome, hypothyroidism. Tachycardia (>100 bpm) in Indian patients commonly due to: fever/infection (very common), anaemia (high prevalence), dehydration, hyperthyroidism, anxiety, atrial fibrillation (AF). Rheumatic heart disease (endemic in India) is a major cause of AF and SVT. Very high HR (>150 bpm) with haemodynamic instability = emergency cardioversion/pharmacological management.
HR.specialist_if_critical_high: Cardiologist (HR >150: SVT, AF with rapid ventricular rate, VT — urgent ECG required)
HR.specialist_if_critical_low: Cardiologist (HR <40: complete heart block, sick sinus — may require pacemaker)
HR.urgency_if_critical_high: immediate if symptomatic (chest pain, syncope, hypotension)
HR.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Elevated heart rate: identify and treat underlying cause (fever, anaemia, dehydration, thyroid disease). Stay hydrated. Avoid caffeine excess. Manage stress. If persistent >100 bpm at rest without obvious cause, see a cardiologist.
HR.source: LOINC 8867-4. AHA normal HR 60–100 bpm. RHD India: Bhaya et al. 2010. Critical HR thresholds: ACLS guidelines 2020.

Ca.note_on_indian_range: Standard serum calcium range (8.5–10.5 mg/dL = 2.12–2.62 mmol/L) is internationally uniform. Calcium must always be corrected for albumin (corrected Ca = measured Ca + 0.8 × [4 – albumin g/dL]). Hypocalcaemia is extremely common in India due to: Vitamin D deficiency (>70–80% of Indians), poor dietary calcium intake (average Indian diet is calcium-deficient), and post-thyroidectomy/parathyroidectomy. Hypercalcaemia in India is commonly from primary hyperparathyroidism, malignancy (PTHrP-related), sarcoidosis, and vitamin D toxicity (widespread unregulated D3 supplementation in India). Severe hypocalcaemia causes tetany (Chvostek's/Trousseau's sign, carpopedal spasm).
Ca.specialist_if_critical_high: Endocrinologist (hypercalcaemia >13 mg/dL — hyperparathyroidism, malignancy workup; >14 = medical emergency: IV fluids + calcitonin)
Ca.specialist_if_critical_low: Endocrinologist / Physician (hypocalcaemia <7 mg/dL = tetany, seizure, QTc prolongation risk — IV calcium gluconate)
Ca.urgency_if_critical_low: immediate if symptomatic (tetany, seizures, ECG changes)
Ca.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low calcium: increase calcium in diet — milk (1 glass = 300 mg Ca), curd, paneer, ragi (nachni, highest plant-based Ca), sesame seeds (til), drumstick leaves (moringa). Ensure adequate Vitamin D (sunlight 15–20 min/day or supplement) as D3 is essential for calcium absorption. Avoid excess oxalate-rich foods (spinach, beet) with calcium-rich foods.
Ca.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम कैल्शियम: दूध, दही, पनीर, रागी, तिल और सहजन (मोरिंगा) खाएं। विटामिन D के लिए रोज 15-20 मिनट धूप लें।
Ca.source: LOINC 17861-6. Standard reference 8.5–10.5 mg/dL (Harrison's 21st ed). Albumin-corrected calcium formula: Payne et al. Lancet 1973. India Vitamin D deficiency: Ritu & Gupta Eur J Clin Nutr 2014. Ragi calcium: NIN India Nutritive Value table.

MMSE.note_on_indian_range: MMSE has well-documented ceiling and floor effects. A key limitation in India is educational bias — MMSE was validated in literate Western populations. Illiterate or less educated Indian patients may score lower on MMSE despite being cognitively intact. The Hindi Mental State Examination (HMSE) and ACE-III (Addenbrooke's Cognitive Examination) with regional language adaptations are preferred for Indian patients. India has 5.3 million people living with dementia (Alzheimer's Disease International 2015), projected to double by 2030. MMSE <24 in an educated adult Indian should trigger formal neuropsychological assessment.
MMSE.specialist_if_critical_low: Neurologist / Geriatrician / Psychiatrist (MMSE <18 = moderate cognitive impairment — dementia workup: MRI brain, B12, TSH, metabolic screen)
MMSE.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low MMSE score: assess for treatable causes — B12 deficiency (very common in vegetarian Indians), thyroid disease, depression, medications. Diet: B12-rich foods (milk, curd, paneer, eggs), omega-3 (flaxseeds, walnuts), turmeric (neuroprotective emerging evidence), and cognitive stimulation activities.
MMSE.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम MMSE: B12 की कमी (शाकाहारियों में आम), थायरॉइड रोग और अवसाद की जांच करवाएं। दूध, दही, अखरोट, अलसी खाएं।
MMSE.source: LOINC 72107-6. Folstein et al. J Psychiatr Res 1975. India educational bias and Hindi MMSE: Ganguli et al. Neuroepidemiology 1995. India dementia prevalence: ADI World Alzheimer Report 2015. ACE-III Indian validation: Mukherjee et al. 2017.

OGTT.display_name: Oral Glucose Tolerance Test (2-Hour Plasma Glucose)
OGTT.note_on_indian_range: Standard 75g OGTT is used for diabetes diagnosis in India — identical thresholds to WHO/ADA (2-hour glucose <140 mg/dL = normal; 140–199 = IGT; ≥200 = diabetes). The OGTT is also used for GDM (gestational diabetes) screening in India using 75g OGTT (DIPSI method): 2-hour glucose ≥140 mg/dL = GDM. India has an immense GDM burden (~10–14% of all pregnancies). The Diabetes in Pregnancy Study Group India (DIPSI) simplified criteria are widely used. The ICMR-INDIAB study found India has 101 million diabetics — IGT screening by OGTT is critical for early intervention.
OGTT.specialist_if_critical_high: Endocrinologist / Diabetologist (OGTT ≥200 mg/dL = diabetes diagnosis; GDM specialist for pregnant women)
OGTT.urgency_if_critical_high: within 1–2 weeks for follow-up and management plan
OGTT.urgency_if_critical_low: immediate (hypoglycaemia during OGTT — give glucose, abort test)
OGTT.urgency_if_watch: within 2–4 weeks (IGT — lifestyle modification intensive programme)
OGTT.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Elevated OGTT: adopt a low-GI Indian diet, increase physical activity, achieve ideal body weight. Replace white rice with brown rice, millets (bajra, jowar, ragi), reduce sweets and fruit juices. Consult a diabetologist for a structured plan.
OGTT.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च OGTT: बाजरा, ज्वार, रागी को अपने आहार में शामिल करें। सफेद चावल और मिठाइयों से परहेज करें। नियमित व्यायाम करें।
OGTT.source: LOINC 14743-9. WHO/ADA 2023 OGTT diagnostic criteria. DIPSI GDM method: Seshiah et al. JAPI 2004. ICMR-INDIAB study Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2023.

ABG_pH.note_on_indian_range: Blood pH 7.35–7.45 is a universal constant across all populations including Indian. pH is tightly regulated — a deviation of ±0.1 pH units has major clinical consequences. Metabolic acidosis (pH <7.35 with low HCO3) is common in India from: sepsis (most common cause in ICU), DKA (India has highest diabetes burden), diarrhoea/cholera (high prevalence). Respiratory acidosis (pH <7.35 with high pCO2) from COPD exacerbations, pneumonia, TB. A pH <7.1 is associated with myocardial depression and cardiac arrhythmias. Critical pH >7.60 (alkalosis) is less common — from nasogastric suction, severe vomiting, over-ventilation.
ABG_pH.specialist_if_critical_high: Intensivist (pH >7.60 = severe alkalosis — hypoventilation treatment, electrolyte correction)
ABG_pH.specialist_if_critical_low: Intensivist / Emergency Physician (pH <7.20 = severe acidosis — immediate cause-directed treatment: bicarbonate, ventilatory support, or dialysis in AKI lactic acidosis)
ABG_pH.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Blood pH abnormality is a critical medical emergency — diet has no role in acute management. Long-term: for metabolic acidosis from CKD, oral sodium bicarbonate supplementation is often prescribed. Maintain hydration and treat underlying infections promptly.
ABG_pH.source: LOINC 2744-1. ABG normal ranges: StatPearls NBK536919. Wikipedia Arterial Blood Gas article. Critical pH <7.20: ACLS 2020. India sepsis-related acidosis: AIIMS ICU protocols.

BSL.note_on_indian_range: Random (casual) blood glucose ≥200 mg/dL with classic diabetic symptoms (polyuria, polydipsia, weight loss) = diabetes diagnosis. In India, point-of-care glucometer readings (BSL by glucometer) are the most commonly performed test in outpatient and inpatient settings. Glucometers can vary in accuracy by ±15–20% from laboratory values; for diagnosis, a lab venous sample is recommended. Hyperosmolar Hyperglycaemic State (HHS) in type 2 DM can present with BSL >600 mg/dL. India-specific: frequent glucose checks during Ramzan/fasting for patients on insulin or sulphonylureas are critical to detect hypoglycaemia.
BSL.specialist_if_critical_high: Endocrinologist / Emergency Physician (BSL >400 = acute hyperglycaemia; >600 with altered consciousness = HHS emergency — ICU, IV insulin, aggressive hydration)
BSL.specialist_if_critical_low: Emergency Physician (BSL <50 = severe hypoglycaemia — give 15–20g glucose: 3–4 glucose tablets, or 4 teaspoons of sugar dissolved in water; if unconscious: IV dextrose 25–50 mL of D50W or IM glucagon 1 mg)
BSL.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low blood sugar emergency: immediately consume fast-acting carbohydrates — 4 teaspoons of sugar dissolved in water, glucose tablets, fruit juice (not diet), or biscuits. Follow with a complex carbohydrate snack (roti with sabzi). If unconscious, do NOT give anything by mouth — call 108 (emergency).
BSL.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम ब्लड शुगर: तुरंत 4 चम्मच चीनी पानी में मिलाकर पिएं, या ग्लूकोज़ की गोली लें। अगर होश न हो तो 108 को कॉल करें।
BSL.source: LOINC 2339-0. ADA Standards 2024 (random glucose ≥200 mg/dL = diabetes). HHS management: ADA 2023 consensus statement. Glucometer accuracy: ISO 15197:2013. India Ramzan fasting and diabetes: Hassanein et al. Diabetes Res Clin Pract 2016.

interpretation.negative: Normal — no detectable blood in stool
interpretation.positive: Abnormal — blood detected; requires follow-up colonoscopy

FOBT.note_on_indian_range: FOBT is a qualitative (positive/negative) test, not a quantitative one. It is the primary colorectal cancer (CRC) screening test globally. Positive FOBT requires diagnostic colonoscopy. In India, CRC is the 8th most common cancer and rising due to dietary changes and urbanisation. Guaiac-based FOBT (gFOBT) can have false positives from red meat, raw broccoli, turnips, or NSAIDs (ibuprofen, aspirin). The Faecal Immunochemical Test (FIT) is more specific for human haemoglobin. Other causes of a positive FOBT in India: haemorrhoids (extremely prevalent), hookworm infestation (common in rural India), gastric ulcer (H. pylori prevalence ~52–82% in India), inflammatory bowel disease, and colorectal polyps. A positive FOBT with anaemia in India warrants urgent colonoscopy and upper GI endoscopy.
FOBT.specialist_if_positive: Gastroenterologist (positive FOBT → diagnostic colonoscopy and/or upper GI endoscopy; if positive with Hb drop — urgent referral within 2 weeks)
FOBT.urgency_if_positive: within 2–4 weeks for asymptomatic positive; immediate if active rectal bleeding or haematochezia
FOBT.indian_diet_tip_positive_en: Positive FOBT: avoid gFOBT confounders before repeat testing — stop red meat, raw broccoli, turnips for 3 days; avoid aspirin/NSAIDs for 7 days. Increase dietary fibre (whole grains, dal, fruits, vegetables) for long-term bowel health. Do NOT dismiss a positive result — arrange a colonoscopy.
FOBT.indian_diet_tip_positive_hi: पॉजिटिव FOBT: जांच दोबारा करने से पहले 3 दिन लाल मांस और कच्ची सब्जियां बंद करें। आंत के स्वास्थ्य के लिए फाइबर युक्त भोजन खाएं। कोलोनोस्कोपी ज़रूर करवाएं।
FOBT.source: LOINC 14563-1. USPSTF CRC screening guidelines 2021. India CRC epidemiology: ICMR 2021. H. pylori India prevalence: Malfertheiner et al. Gut 2022. FIT vs gFOBT: WHO CRC screening guidelines 2022. Hookworm India: ICMR parasitic disease burden.

PCT.note_on_indian_range: No significant Indian ethnic variation documented. ISCCM (Indian Society of Critical Care Medicine) and AIIMS ICU protocols use the same international cutoffs. PCT has replaced CRP as the primary sepsis biomarker in Indian tertiary-care ICUs.
PCT.specialist_if_critical_high: Intensivist / Infectious Disease Specialist (severe sepsis or septic shock workup)
PCT.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High PCT signals bacterial infection or sepsis — this is an emergency. Do NOT self-treat. Seek hospital care immediately. Stay hydrated with ORS or coconut water only if safe to drink.
PCT.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च PCT जीवाणु संक्रमण या सेप्सिस का संकेत है — यह आपातकालीन स्थिति है। खुद दवा न लें। तुरंत अस्पताल जाएं। यदि पीना सुरक्षित हो तो ORS या नारियल पानी पिएं।
PCT.source: Müller B et al. Critical Care 2010. Schuetz P et al. JAMA 2009. ISCCM Sepsis India guidelines. LOINC 33959-8 (Procalcitonin serum/plasma by immunoassay). No Indian ethnic variation identified.

LH.note_on_indian_range: ICMR-PCOS Task Force study (Lancet Reg Health SE Asia 2023, PMID 37090159) established Indian reproductive-aged women's hormonal reference intervals. LH in Indian women follicular phase is consistent with international ranges. PCOS is highly prevalent in India (~9% per ICMR), causing elevated LH:FSH ratio. Indian labs (SRL, Thyrocare) report the same ranges.
LH.specialist_if_critical_high: Gynecologist / Reproductive Endocrinologist (PCOS, premature ovarian failure, pituitary tumour)
LH.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High LH in women may indicate PCOS or early menopause. Maintain healthy weight — obesity worsens LH imbalance. Eat whole grains (bajra, jowar), dal, sabzi. Avoid excess sugar and refined carbs. Yoga and weight management help normalise hormones.
LH.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: महिलाओं में उच्च LH PCOS या रजोनिवृत्ति का संकेत हो सकता है। स्वस्थ वजन बनाए रखें। बाजरा, ज्वार, दाल, सब्जी खाएं। चीनी और मैदा कम करें। वजन नियंत्रण और योग से हार्मोन संतुलन होता है।
LH.source: Mayo Clinic Labs LH reference intervals. ICMR-PCOS Task Force 2023 (Lancet Reg Health SE Asia, PMID 37090159). LOINC 10501-5.

FSH.note_on_indian_range: ICMR-PCOS Task Force study (2023) confirmed Indian women's FSH is consistent with international ranges. Day-3 FSH >10 mIU/mL is used by Indian IVF centres as diminished ovarian reserve threshold. Post-menopausal FSH is markedly elevated (>25 mIU/mL). Indian labs use manufacturer-supplied reference ranges.
FSH.specialist_if_critical_high: Gynecologist / Reproductive Endocrinologist (premature ovarian failure, menopause evaluation, ovarian reserve workup)
FSH.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High FSH (day-3) suggests reduced ovarian reserve. Eat antioxidant-rich foods: amla, spinach, walnuts, flaxseeds, and sesame seeds (til). Avoid smoking and alcohol. Stress reduction through yoga may improve hormonal balance.
FSH.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च FSH (तीसरे दिन) डिम्बग्रंथि भंडार में कमी का संकेत हो सकता है। आंवला, पालक, अखरोट, अलसी, तिल खाएं। धूम्रपान और शराब से बचें। योग से तनाव कम करें।
FSH.source: Mayo Clinic Labs FSH reference intervals. ICMR-PCOS Task Force 2023 (PMID 37090159). LOINC 15067-2.

TG.note_on_indian_range: Apollo multicentric Indian study (PMC4062657, 10,665 subjects) showed higher upper TG reference limits in Indians vs Western populations due to higher carbohydrate dietary intake. ICMR-INDIAB-17 (Lancet Diab Endocrinol 2023, n=113,043): dyslipidemia prevalence 81.2% in India; high TG uniformly prevalent across all regions. Lipid Association of India (LAI) Expert Consensus 2020: Desirable TG <150 mg/dL; borderline 150-199 mg/dL; high ≥200 mg/dL.
TG.specialist_if_critical_high: Cardiologist / Endocrinologist (pancreatitis risk at ≥500 mg/dL; cardiovascular risk assessment)
TG.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24-48 hours if ≥500 mg/dL (acute pancreatitis risk)
TG.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Reduce refined carbs, white rice, maida, sweets, and fried snacks. Replace with roti, jowar, bajra, and brown rice. Eat more dal and sabzi. Avoid alcohol completely. Exercise 30 min daily. Omega-3 from flaxseeds, walnuts, and fatty fish effectively lowers TG.
TG.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: मैदा, मिठाई, तली हुई चीजें और शराब कम करें। रोटी, बाजरा, ज्वार, दाल, सब्जी खाएं। अलसी, अखरोट और मछली से ओमेगा-3 लें। रोज 30 मिनट व्यायाम करें।
TG.source: NCEP ATP III guidelines. Lipid Association of India (LAI) Expert Consensus 2020. ICMR-INDIAB-17 (Lancet Diab Endocrinol 2023, PMID 37178745). Apollo Indian reference study PMC4062657. LOINC 2571-8.

DBP.note_on_indian_range: CSI (Cardiological Society of India) 2020 guidelines: Target DBP <80 mmHg for most adults; <70 mmHg may be acceptable in elderly. India has ~30% adult hypertension prevalence (ICMR-INDIAB). DBP >90 mmHg warrants antihypertensive treatment per Indian guidelines. Isolated diastolic hypertension is common in young Indian adults aged 20-40.
DBP.specialist_if_critical_high: Cardiologist / Emergency Physician (hypertensive emergency — end-organ damage evaluation required)
DBP.specialist_if_critical_low: Emergency Physician (haemodynamic shock — septic, cardiogenic, or haemorrhagic)
DBP.urgency_if_critical_high: immediate if symptomatic; within 24h if asymptomatic
DBP.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Reduce salt to <5g/day. Avoid pickles, papads, packaged namkeen, and excessive chai. Eat more vegetables, low-fat dairy (chaas, dahi), and fruits. Reduce ghee and saturated fat. Yoga and pranayama are proven to help control blood pressure in Indian studies.
DBP.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: नमक 5 ग्राम/दिन से कम करें। अचार, पापड़, पैकेज्ड नमकीन से बचें। सब्जियां, दही, छाछ, फल खाएं। घी कम करें। योग और प्राणायाम से रक्तचाप नियंत्रण में मदद मिलती है।
DBP.source: CSI Hypertension Guidelines 2020. JNC 8 (JAMA 2014;311:507). ICMR-INDIAB hypertension data. LOINC 8462-4.

MPV.note_on_indian_range: Indian labs (SRL Diagnostics, Dr Lal PathLabs) report MPV normal range as 7.0–12.0 fL or 7.5–12.5 fL. No significant Indian ethnic variation documented. High MPV (>12 fL) is associated with platelet activation and increased cardiovascular and metabolic risk — relevant given India's high CVD and diabetes burden. Low MPV (<7.5 fL) seen in aplastic anemia, Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome, and iron-deficiency states.
MPV.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High MPV signals platelet activation linked to cardiovascular risk. Eat omega-3 rich foods: flaxseed (alsi), walnuts (akhrot), and fatty fish. Avoid high saturated fat and trans-fat foods. Stay physically active. Controlling diabetes and blood pressure also helps normalise MPV.
MPV.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च MPV हृदय जोखिम से जुड़ा है। अलसी, अखरोट और मछली खाएं। संतृप्त वसा और ट्रांस फैट कम करें। शारीरिक रूप से सक्रिय रहें। मधुमेह और रक्तचाप को नियंत्रित करें।
MPV.source: Maconi A et al. J Clin Lab Anal 2020. SRL Diagnostics India reference ranges. LOINC 32623-4.

CD4.note_on_indian_range: India-specific data from NACO Annual Report 2022-23: median CD4 at ART initiation in India is ~250 cells/µL (late presentation common). Baseline CD4 in HIV-negative healthy Indians may average 600-900 cells/µL. NACO India ART Guidelines 2021: initiate ART for ALL HIV-positive individuals regardless of CD4 (universal test-and-treat). CD4 <200 = start PCP and Cryptococcal prophylaxis. CD4 <100 = start MAC prophylaxis per NACO.
CD4.specialist_if_critical_low: Infectious Disease Specialist / Government ART Centre (CD4 <200 = AIDS stage; immediate prophylaxis and ART review)
CD4.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low CD4 means weakened immunity. Eat protein-rich foods: eggs, dal, paneer, sprouts, soya. Avoid raw or undercooked meat, unfiltered water, and unpasteurized dairy. Take ALL prescribed ART medications at the same time daily — never miss a dose.
CD4.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम CD4 कमजोर प्रतिरोधक क्षमता का संकेत है। अंडे, दाल, पनीर, अंकुरित अनाज, सोया खाएं। कच्चा मांस, असुरक्षित पानी और कच्चे दूध से बचें। ART दवाएं रोज एक ही समय पर नियमित लें।
CD4.source: WHO HIV Staging Criteria. NACO ART Guidelines India 2021. NACO Annual Report 2022-23. LOINC 24467-3.

CEA.note_on_indian_range: Indian labs (SRL, Dr Lal, Thyrocare) use non-smoker cutoff <2.5 ng/mL and smoker cutoff <5.0 ng/mL, consistent with international standards. National Cancer Grid (NCG) India recommends CEA for colorectal cancer post-surgical monitoring and recurrence detection. Not used as a primary screening tool. CEA >20 ng/mL strongly suggests active malignancy; >100 ng/mL indicates widespread metastatic disease.
CEA.specialist_if_critical_high: Oncologist / Gastroenterologist (colorectal cancer workup — colonoscopy and CT scan required)
CEA.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Elevated CEA warrants urgent cancer evaluation — diet alone cannot lower CEA if malignancy is present. Eat a cancer-protective diet: high-fiber vegetables, fruits, and whole grains. Minimize red and processed meat. Quit tobacco and alcohol immediately.
CEA.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: बढ़ा हुआ CEA कैंसर जांच का संकेत है — तुरंत डॉक्टर से मिलें। उच्च फाइबर भोजन, फल, सब्जियां, साबुत अनाज खाएं। लाल मांस, तंबाकू और शराब से बचें।
CEA.source: ASCO/NCCN Colorectal Cancer Guidelines. National Cancer Grid India. Fletcher RH. J Clin Oncol 1986;4(4):627. LOINC 2000-8.

CA125.note_on_indian_range: All Indian labs use <35 U/mL as the universal cutoff (consistent with international standard). CA-125 is elevated in non-malignant conditions common in India: endometriosis, pelvic inflammatory disease (PID), and peritonitis — clinical context is essential. National Cancer Grid (NCG) India uses CA-125 for ovarian cancer monitoring, not primary screening. Post-menopausal women with CA-125 >35 U/mL have significantly higher malignancy risk.
CA125.specialist_if_critical_high: Gynecologist / Oncologist (ovarian cancer workup — transvaginal ultrasound and CT scan required)
CA125.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Elevated CA-125 needs urgent gynaecological evaluation. Maintain a healthy weight — obesity raises CA-125. Eat omega-3 rich foods, fruits, and vegetables. Exercise regularly. Women above 40 should have annual gynaecological check-ups.
CA125.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: बढ़ा हुआ CA-125 स्त्री रोग विशेषज्ञ से तत्काल जांच की आवश्यकता है। स्वस्थ वजन बनाए रखें। ओमेगा-3, फल और सब्जियां खाएं। 40 से अधिक आयु की महिलाएं सालाना स्त्री रोग जांच करवाएं।
CA125.source: FIGO Ovarian Cancer Guidelines. National Cancer Grid India. ACOG Committee Opinion on Adnexal Mass. LOINC 10334-1.

hCG.note_on_indian_range: Same international reference ranges used in India. β-hCG is used for: early pregnancy confirmation, ectopic pregnancy diagnosis (non-rising hCG + pain), gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) monitoring, and Down syndrome screening (combined with NT + PAPP-A). FOGSI (Federation of Obstetric and Gynaecological Societies of India) ANC guidelines use the same hCG reference tables. hCG >100,000 mIU/mL in a non-pregnant person is strongly suggestive of GTD or germ-cell tumour.
hCG.specialist_if_critical_high: Gynaecologist (non-pregnant with elevated hCG: rule out gestational trophoblastic disease, germ cell tumour)
hCG.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24-48 hours if non-pregnant with elevated hCG
hCG.indian_diet_tip_high_en: If unexpectedly high hCG in a non-pregnant person, seek urgent medical evaluation. During normal pregnancy, eat a balanced diet rich in iron (palak, rajma), folate (green vegetables, dal), calcium (dahi, milk), and protein. Take all prescribed prenatal supplements.
hCG.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: यदि गर्भवती नहीं हैं और hCG अधिक है, तो तुरंत डॉक्टर से मिलें। गर्भावस्था में पालक, राजमा, हरी सब्जियां, दाल, दही, दूध खाएं और प्रसवपूर्व सप्लीमेंट लें।
hCG.source: FOGSI ANC Guidelines. Braunstein GD. N Engl J Med 1987;316:444. LOINC 21198-7.

SaO2.note_on_indian_range: Standard international reference ranges apply in India. SaO2 is measured via arterial blood gas (invasive), distinct from SpO2 (non-invasive pulse oximetry). In COPD patients — India has ~6.4% COPD prevalence (GOLD/ICMR) — chronic SaO2 of 88-92% may be acceptable baseline. Long-term oxygen therapy (LTOT) is indicated when resting SaO2 <88% on two measurements ≥3 weeks apart per Indian chest guidelines.
SaO2.specialist_if_critical_low: Pulmonologist / Intensivist (respiratory failure, ARDS, pulmonary embolism)
SaO2.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low oxygen saturation is a medical emergency — seek immediate hospital care. If chronic respiratory disease, quit smoking immediately. Indoor air quality from biomass cooking affects ~60% of rural Indian women; switching to LPG/PNG significantly improves lung health.
SaO2.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम ऑक्सीजन संतृप्ति आपात स्थिति है — तुरंत अस्पताल जाएं। धूम्रपान तुरंत बंद करें। बायोमास खाना पकाने के धुएं से बचें — LPG/PNG पर स्विच करें।
SaO2.source: GOLD COPD Guidelines 2024. WHO oxygen therapy guidelines. Indian Chest Society COPD guidelines. LOINC 2708-6.

PEFR.note_on_indian_range: Indian predicted PEFR values are 15-20% lower than Caucasian values due to smaller thoracic dimensions. Aggarwal AN et al. (Indian J Chest Dis Allied Sci 2017) established Indian-specific PEFR norms: Adult Indian male mean ~450-500 L/min; adult Indian female mean ~320-380 L/min (varies by height and age). GINA asthma guidelines are adapted for Indian practice by the Indian Chest Society and AAPAAACI. Always use Indian PEFR prediction equations — not Caucasian norms — when interpreting results in Indian patients.
PEFR.specialist_if_critical_low: Pulmonologist (severe asthma exacerbation or COPD; PEFR <150 L/min = life-threatening asthma)
PEFR.urgency_if_critical_low: immediate if <150 L/min; within 24h if 150-250 L/min
PEFR.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low PEFR suggests airflow obstruction. Avoid smoke, dust, and indoor cooking smoke. Use prescribed inhalers regularly without skipping doses. Breathing exercises and pranayama (anulom vilom) improve lung function over time. Avoid cold drinks during asthma exacerbations.
PEFR.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: कम PEFR अस्थमा/COPD का संकेत है। धुएं, धूल और घर के प्रदूषण से बचें। निर्धारित इनहेलर नियमित उपयोग करें। अनुलोम-विलोम और प्राणायाम फेफड़ों को मजबूत करते हैं।
PEFR.source: Aggarwal AN et al. Indian PEFR norms, Indian J Chest Dis 2017. GINA 2024 asthma guidelines. LOINC 19935-1.

PTH.note_on_indian_range: India has extremely high vitamin D deficiency prevalence (~70-90% of urban adults per AIIMS and ICMR data). This causes secondary hyperparathyroidism; healthy Indian adults with vitamin D deficiency may show PTH 65-88 pg/mL before treatment. AIIMS and PGIMER Chandigarh studies report reference range 18-88 pg/mL as contextually appropriate for Indians. Standard international range 15-65 pg/mL is the target. Supplementation with vitamin D3 typically normalises PTH within 3-6 months.
PTH.specialist_if_critical_high: Endocrinologist / Nephrologist (hyperparathyroidism, CKD-related bone mineral disease evaluation)
PTH.specialist_if_critical_low: Endocrinologist (hypoparathyroidism — check post-thyroid/parathyroid surgery; tetany risk)
PTH.urgency_if_critical_low: within 24-48 hours (tetany and seizure risk if calcium also low)
PTH.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High PTH often means low vitamin D or calcium. Get sunlight daily (15-20 min morning sun, before 10 AM). Eat calcium-rich foods: ragi (nachni), sesame seeds (til), dahi, milk, and green leafy vegetables. Vitamin D3 supplements are often needed in Indians — consult your doctor.
PTH.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च PTH अक्सर विटामिन D या कैल्शियम की कमी का संकेत है। रोज सुबह 15-20 मिनट धूप लें। रागी, तिल, दही, दूध, हरी पत्तेदार सब्जियां खाएं। भारतीयों को अक्सर विटामिन D3 सप्लीमेंट की आवश्यकता होती है — डॉक्टर से पूछें।
PTH.source: KDIGO CKD-MBD Guidelines 2017. Marwaha RK et al. AIIMS vitamin D study (J Assoc Physicians India). LOINC 2731-8.

ACTH.note_on_indian_range: Same as international ranges. Diurnal variation is essential: always measure at 8 AM fasting for meaningful interpretation. In India, tuberculosis-related adrenal insufficiency (TB affecting adrenal glands) is more common than autoimmune Addison's disease (unlike Western countries). ACTH >200 pg/mL with low cortisol = Addison's disease until proven otherwise. ACTH stimulation test (Synacthen/cosyntropin test) is the gold standard for adrenal insufficiency in Indian clinical practice.
ACTH.specialist_if_critical_high: Endocrinologist (Addison's disease / ectopic ACTH syndrome — exclude TB adrenals and lung cancer)
ACTH.specialist_if_critical_low: Endocrinologist (secondary adrenal insufficiency from pituitary failure; Cushing's syndrome from exogenous steroids)
ACTH.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High ACTH needs specialist evaluation urgently. Reduce chronic stress — a major ACTH driver. Eat a balanced anti-inflammatory diet: vegetables, dal, whole grains. Get 7-8 hours of sleep daily. Avoid alcohol. Consider yoga and mindfulness.
ACTH.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च ACTH के लिए तुरंत विशेषज्ञ से मिलें। तनाव कम करें, 7-8 घंटे की नींद लें, शराब से बचें। सब्जियां, दाल, साबुत अनाज खाएं। योग और ध्यान से मदद मिलती है।
ACTH.source: Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guidelines: Adrenal Insufficiency 2016. AIIMS endocrinology protocols. LOINC 2141-0.

BA.note_on_units: Indian labs (SRL, Lal PathLabs, Thyrocare, Metropolis) report basophils in BOTH absolute count (/µL or /cumm) AND as percentage (%). Most commonly reported as percentage in Indian DLC (Differential Leucocyte Count) results — typically printed as 'Basophils: 0–1%'. Automated analyzers (Sysmex XN, ABX Micros) now report absolute count alongside. Basophil counting by automated hematology analyzer is notoriously inaccurate due to low cell numbers — flow cytometry is more precise but rarely done clinically. Never diagnose basophilia on a single automated count without peripheral smear review.
BA.note_on_indian_range: Indian labs universally use 0–1% and 0–200/µL as normal range for basophils. This is consistent with international reference ranges. Basophilia (>1% or >200/µL) is uncommon and clinically significant in India due to high prevalence of: (1) Chronic Myeloid Leukemia — incidence 1–2/100,000 in India, where basophilia >5% is a diagnostic criterion; (2) Tuberculosis — can cause mild basophilia; (3) Hypothyroidism — common in Indian women (6.6 million cases, India Thyroid Society estimate). Isolated basophilia on an automated count without confirmatory smear is common pre-analytical noise.
causes_of_high_ba: Chronic Myeloid Leukemia (CML) — hallmark finding, >5% basophils
BA.specialist_if_critical_high: Hematologist — CML and myeloproliferative disease workup urgent; order BCR-ABL1 molecular test, karyotype
BA.specialist_if_watch_high: Physician (rule out hypothyroidism with TSH, confirm with peripheral smear)
BA.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24–48 hours (basophilia >5% — rule out CML urgently)
BA.indian_diet_tip_high_en: A mildly high basophil count may indicate allergy or thyroid issues. Avoid known allergens. Eat an anti-inflammatory diet: haldi (turmeric) daal, amla, ginger. Do not self-medicate. See a doctor for further tests.
BA.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: बेसोफिल की हल्की वृद्धि एलर्जी या थायरॉइड की समस्या का संकेत हो सकती है। ज्ञात एलर्जेन से बचें। हल्दी वाली दाल, आंवला और अदरक खाएं। खुद दवा न लें — डॉक्टर से मिलें।
BA.indian_diet_tip_high_mr: बेसोफिल थोडे वाढलेले असल्यास ऍलर्जी किंवा थायरॉइड समस्या असू शकते. ज्ञात ऍलर्जेन टाळा. हळद घातलेली डाळ, आवळा आणि आले खा. स्वतः औषध घेऊ नका — डॉक्टरांना भेटा.
BA.source: LOINC 704-7 (absolute automated), 706-2 (% automated), 705-4 (absolute manual), 707-0 (% manual). Normal 0–200/µL, 0–1%: StatPearls NBK604207. Flow cytometry reference: PMID 16197480 (95% CI 0.22–1.28%). CML basophilia >5%: WHO Classification of Myeloid Neoplasms 2022. India CML incidence: Indian Journal of Medical Research 2013. Hypothyroidism India 6.6 million: India Thyroid Society. Critical high >1000/µL: CLSI / Mayo Clinic.

PT.note_on_units: PT is reported in seconds AND as INR in all Indian labs. INR (International Normalized Ratio) = (Patient PT / Mean Normal PT)^ISI, where ISI is the International Sensitivity Index of the thromboplastin reagent. INR standardizes results across labs — always use INR when interpreting anticoagulation therapy. PT in seconds alone is lab-specific and cannot be compared across different labs. In India, INR is the clinically actionable number. PT activity (%) is additionally reported in some older Indian labs — normal is 70–100%.
PT.note_on_indian_range: Indian labs (SRL, Lal PathLabs, Thyrocare, Metropolis, government hospital labs) report PT 11–13.5 seconds with INR 0.8–1.2 as normal. The most clinically common scenario in India: patients on warfarin (Acitrom/acenocoumarol) for atrial fibrillation or mechanical heart valves — target INR 2.0–3.0, checked monthly. Acenocoumarol (Acitrom) is more widely used than warfarin in India due to lower cost. INR monitoring of Acitrom patients is a high-volume test at Indian labs. Pre-operative PT/INR >1.5 is considered abnormal at most Indian surgical centers. Critical INR >5.0 = high bleeding risk requiring urgent management.
PT.pathway: Extrinsic pathway + Common pathway of coagulation
causes_of_prolonged_pt: Liver disease / cirrhosis (reduced clotting factor synthesis)
Congenital factor deficiency (II, V, VII, X, fibrinogen)
Malabsorption (Crohn's, celiac) causing Vitamin K malabsorption
causes_of_short_pt: Thrombosis / hypercoagulable state (rarely causes short PT)
drug_interferences: Warfarin, Acenocoumarol — prolongs PT significantly; therapeutic goal
Direct Oral Anticoagulants (DOACs: rivaroxaban, apixaban) — variable effect, PT NOT reliable for monitoring DOACs
Heparin (unfractionated) — minimally prolongs PT at low doses
Antibiotics (prolonged use) — reduce gut Vitamin K synthesis, prolongs PT
Aspirin / NSAIDs — no direct effect on PT but synergistic bleeding risk
PT.specimen_requirements: Light blue top (sodium citrate 3.2%) tube. Must be filled to exact line — under-fill alters citrate:blood ratio and falsely prolongs PT. Test within 4 hours at room temperature or freeze at -70°C.
PT.specialist_if_critical_high_inr: Emergency Physician / Hematologist — INR >5.0 with no bleeding: omit anticoagulant dose, monitor. INR >8.0 or active bleeding: IV Vitamin K + Prothrombin Complex Concentrate (PCC) or Fresh Frozen Plasma (FFP)
PT.specialist_if_watch: Physician / Cardiologist if on anticoagulation therapy. Hepatologist if liver disease suspected.
PT.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24 hours if asymptomatic; IMMEDIATE if active bleeding
PT.indian_diet_tip_high_en: A high PT/INR means your blood takes longer to clot — often from blood-thinning medication or liver disease. If you are on blood thinners, do not change your dose yourself. Eat consistent amounts of green leafy vegetables (not suddenly more or less) — spinach and methi affect Vitamin K which interacts with blood thinners.
PT.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च PT/INR का मतलब है खून जमने में अधिक समय लग रहा है। यह खून पतला करने वाली दवाओं या लिवर की बीमारी से हो सकता है। खुद दवा की मात्रा न बदलें। हरी पत्तेदार सब्जियां (पालक, मेथी) की मात्रा में अचानक बड़ा बदलाव न करें — ये Vitamin K से भरपूर हैं जो दवा पर असर डालती हैं।
