PT.indian_diet_tip_high_mr: उच्च PT/INR म्हणजे रक्त गोठण्यास जास्त वेळ लागतो. रक्त पातळ करण्याची औषधे किंवा यकृत रोगामुळे होऊ शकते. स्वतःहून औषधाचा डोस बदलू नका. पालक, मेथी यांसारख्या हिरव्या पालेभाज्या अचानक जास्त किंवा कमी खाऊ नका — त्या Vitamin K ने समृद्ध असतात.
PT.source: LOINC 5902-2 (PT seconds), 6301-6 (INR). INR formula: WHO ISI calibration system. Normal 11–13.5 sec, INR 0.8–1.2: ARUP, MLabs (9.8–12.7 sec), Wikipedia Prothrombin time. Warfarin therapeutic ranges: American College of Chest Physicians (ACCP) guidelines. Critical INR >5.0: MLabs, ARUP critical value policy. Acitrom (acenocoumarol) India prevalence: Indian Heart Journal. Factors tested: Labcorp 005199. INR NOT appropriate for DOACs: LOINC LP17102-2 definition. Specimen handling: CLSI H21-A5.

BE.note_on_units: BE is reported in mmol/L (SI) or mEq/L (conventional) — numerically equivalent: 1 mmol/L = 1 mEq/L. BE is a CALCULATED parameter derived from measured pH and pCO2 using the Siggaard-Andersen nomogram; it is not directly measured. Two variants: Actual BE (ABE) = whole blood; Standard BE (SBE) = corrected to Hb of 5 g/dL representing extracellular fluid compartment. SBE is preferred in most clinical settings. Most ABG analyzers (Radiometer, Abbott i-STAT, GEM series) in Indian ICUs report SBE automatically. Negative BE = Base Deficit = metabolic acidosis. Positive BE = metabolic alkalosis.
BE.note_on_indian_range: All Indian ICUs, emergency departments, and NICUs use BE as part of ABG (Arterial Blood Gas) analysis. -2 to +2 mmol/L is the universally accepted normal range in India. Key clinical scenarios in Indian ICU practice: (1) DKA (Diabetic Ketoacidosis) — extremely common in India's 77 million diabetics, BE typically -10 to -25 mmol/L; (2) Septic shock — BE <-6 mmol/L indicates poor perfusion (lactate proxy); (3) Severe diarrheal dehydration — common in pediatric and adult Indian patients, BE -5 to -15 mmol/L due to HCO3 loss; (4) NICU — neonatal hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy, BE <-12 mmol/L is Sarnat criteria threshold. Neonatal normal BE is more negative (-10 to -2) in first 48h.

severe.meaning: Severe metabolic acidosis — CRITICAL, urgent intervention
Severe metabolic alkalosis — investigate vomiting, diuretic use, Cushing's

causes_of_base_deficit_negative_be: Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA) — most common in India
Severe diarrhea (bicarbonate loss — very common in India)
Acute Kidney Injury / Renal tubular acidosis
causes_of_base_excess_positive_be: Excessive diuretic use (furosemide, thiazides — hypokalemic alkalosis)
Excessive oral alkali ingestion (antacids, sodium bicarbonate)
BE.not_standalone_note: BE is ALWAYS interpreted as part of the complete ABG. It cannot be assessed in isolation. Always correlate with pH, pCO2, and HCO3. BE quantifies the metabolic component of acid-base disturbance, independent of respiratory contribution.
BE.specialist_if_critical_low: ICU Physician / Emergency Medicine — treat underlying cause; severe BE deficit may require IV sodium bicarbonate or fluid resuscitation
BE.specialist_if_critical_high: ICU Physician — treat underlying cause (replace potassium, stop diuretics, treat vomiting)
BE.urgency_if_critical: IMMEDIATE — ABG is performed in acute/ICU setting; critical BE values are managed in real time
BE.indian_diet_tip_general_en: Base excess is measured in ICU or emergency settings. It is not a routine blood test. If you are admitted to hospital with breathing difficulty, diabetic crisis, or severe dehydration, doctors use this value to guide treatment. Focus on staying hydrated with ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts) if you have diarrhea — this directly helps correct base deficit.
BE.indian_diet_tip_general_hi: बेस एक्सेस ICU या आपातकाल में मापा जाता है — यह नियमित रक्त परीक्षण नहीं है। यदि आपको गंभीर उल्टी-दस्त हो तो ORS (ओरल रिहाइड्रेशन साल्ट) पिएं — यह सीधे बेस की कमी को ठीक करने में मदद करता है।
BE.indian_diet_tip_general_mr: बेस एक्सेस ICU किंवा आपत्कालीन परिस्थितीत मोजले जाते — हे नियमित रक्त परीक्षण नाही. गंभीर जुलाब झाल्यास ORS प्या — हे थेट बेसची कमतरता दूर करण्यास मदत करते.
BE.source: LOINC 1925-7 (arterial), 1926-5 (venous), 1927-3 (standard/SBE). Definition: Astrup & Siggaard-Andersen 1958, Scand J Clin Lab Invest 9:122-132. Normal -2 to +2 mmol/L: Wikipedia Base Excess, Pulsenotes, StatPearls NBK536919, PMC9304040. DKA India: 77 million diabetics — IDF Diabetes Atlas 2021. Neonatal BE Sarnat criteria HIE: threshold <-12 mmol/L. Pediatric diarrhea acidosis India: ICMR national burden study. Formula: BE = 0.93 × ([HCO3] - 24.4 + 14.8 × (pH - 7.4)).

HbF.note_on_indian_range: In the Indian context, HbF levels are critical for screening Beta-Thalassemia and Sickle Cell Disease (SCD), which are prevalent in certain tribal and ethnic clusters (e.g., Central India, Odisha, Maharashtra). In many Indian lab panels, an adult HbF > 2.0% is flagged for further HPLC or electrophoresis testing to rule out Thalassemia minor or HPFH (Hereditary Persistence of Fetal Hemoglobin).
HbF.specialist_if_critical_high: Hematologist / Genetic Counselor (suspected Hemoglobinopathy or HPFH)
HbF.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High HbF is usually a genetic trait or a response to blood disorders. Diet does not reduce it. If linked to Thalassemia, avoid iron-rich foods like excess red meat unless advised by a doctor. Focus on a balanced diet with plenty of folic acid from leafy greens.
HbF.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च HbF आमतौर पर एक आनुवंशिक गुण है। यदि यह थैलेसीमिया से जुड़ा है, तो डॉक्टर की सलाह के बिना आयरन युक्त भोजन (जैसे लाल मांस) से बचें। हरी पत्तेदार सब्जियों से फोलिक एसिड प्राप्त करें।
HbF.source: Standard reference ranges via Mayo Clinic Laboratories and ICMR (Indian Council of Medical Research) guidelines for Hemoglobinopathies. Critical values based on diagnostic thresholds for HPFH and Beta-Thalassemia intermedia. HbF is often therapeutically increased in Sickle Cell patients using Hydroxyurea.

HbS.note_on_indian_range: HbS is a major public health concern in India, particularly in the 'Sickle Cell Belt' (Central, Western, and Southern tribal regions). According to the National Sickle Cell Anemia Elimination Mission 2023, prevalence of the sickle cell trait in tribal populations can range from 1% to as high as 35% in specific communities in Odisha, Madhya Pradesh, and Chhattisgarh. Adult normal should be 0%; any presence of HbS is considered abnormal and requires HPLC confirmation.
HbS.specialist_if_critical_high: Hematologist / Genetic Counselor (management of Sickle Cell Crisis or SCD diagnosis)
HbS.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Presence of HbS requires high hydration (3-4 liters of water daily) to prevent cell sickling. Increase intake of folic acid-rich foods like spinach, lentils (dal), and beans. Avoid extreme cold and high altitudes. Consult a hematologist immediately if you experience severe bone pain.
HbS.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: HbS की उपस्थिति में सेल को सिकुड़ने से रोकने के लिए खूब पानी (रोजाना 3-4 लीटर) पिएं। पालक, दाल और बीन्स जैसे फोलिक एसिड युक्त खाद्य पदार्थ खाएं। तेज दर्द होने पर तुरंत डॉक्टर से संपर्क करें।
HbS.source: National Health Mission (NHM) India, Sickle Cell Disease Guidelines. Normal adult HbS is 0%. Values >0 indicate either Sickle Cell Trait (HbAS) or Disease (HbSS). Critical high (>10% in a previously negative patient) or high percentages in neonates found via HPLC/Electrophoresis warrant urgent hematological follow-up (ICMR/IAP guidelines).

HbC.note_on_indian_range: HbC is rare in the native Indian population compared to HbS, HbE, or HbD. However, it is occasionally detected in urban clinical screenings due to global migration or in compound heterozygous forms (like HbSC disease) in Western and Central India. Standard Indian lab reference for a healthy adult is 0%. Any detected amount via HPLC should be clinically correlated with a peripheral blood smear for 'target cells' and 'HbC crystals'.
HbC.specialist_if_critical_high: Hematologist (to differentiate between Trait, Disease, or SC Compound Heterozygosity)
HbC.urgency_if_critical_high: within 1–2 weeks (usually asymptomatic unless compound with HbS)
HbC.indian_diet_tip_high_en: HbC disease can cause mild chronic hemolysis (breakdown of red cells). Ensure adequate intake of folic acid (Dal, leafy greens like Palak/Methi) to support new red cell production. If the spleen is enlarged, avoid heavy contact sports. Unlike iron-deficiency anemia, do not take iron supplements unless a deficiency is proven by a Ferritin test.
HbC.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: HbC की उपस्थिति में शरीर को नई लाल रक्त कोशिकाएं बनाने के लिए फोलिक एसिड (दाल, पालक, मेथी) की आवश्यकता होती है। जब तक डॉक्टर न कहें, आयरन की गोलियां न लें।
HbC.source: Orphanet clinical database for Hemoglobinopathies; Mayo Clinic Laboratories Reference Catalog. Prevalence data in India noted as rare/incidental (IJMPR 2026/ICMR). Diagnosis primarily via HPLC or Hemoglobin Electrophoresis at alkaline pH.

default_range.note: Diagnosis based on Rotterdam Criteria (2 of 3): Irregular periods, Hyperandrogenism, or Polycystic ovaries on USG.

PCOS.note_on_indian_range: Prevalence in India is high, affecting 10% to 22.5% of women depending on the region. Indian women often present with PCOS at a lower BMI compared to Western counterparts, exhibiting 'Lean PCOS' but with significant insulin resistance. South Indian studies show a higher prevalence of metabolic syndrome comorbidities. Indian labs typically flag Total Testosterone > 60-70 ng/dL and AMH > 5-7 ng/mL as high indicators for PCOS in reproductive-aged women.
PCOS.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Focus on a Low-Glycemic Index (GI) diet to manage insulin. Replace white rice with brown rice, ragi, or oats. Include lean protein like Moong Dal and Chana. Avoid sugary sweets, maida-based snacks, and carbonated drinks. Daily physical activity (30-60 mins) is essential to improve insulin sensitivity.
PCOS.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: इंसुलिन को नियंत्रित करने के लिए लो-ग्लाइसेमिक इंडेक्स (GI) आहार लें। सफेद चावल की जगह ब्राउन राइस, रागी या ओट्स का उपयोग करें। मूंग दाल और चना जैसे प्रोटीन शामिल करें। मीठा, मैदा और कोल्ड ड्रिंक्स से बचें। रोज़ाना 30-60 मिनट व्यायाम बहुत ज़रूरी है।
PCOS.source: Rotterdam Criteria (2003/2023 update); National Health Mission (NHM) India PCOS Guidelines. Prevalence stats: Indian Journal of Endocrinology and Metabolism (approx. 1 in 5 women in India). Lab markers based on standard ICMR reference intervals for Indian women.

CAH.note_on_indian_range: In India, CAH is one of the most common inherited metabolic disorders. While Western incidence is 1 in 15,000, some Indian multicentric studies suggest a higher regional prevalence of approximately 1 in 5,762 births. Indian labs (e.g., Metropolis, Lal PathLabs) typically use 17-OHP as the first-tier screen. Elevated levels in adult Indian women are frequently investigated alongside PCOS to rule out Non-Classic CAH (NC-CAH).
CAH.specialist_if_critical_high: Pediatric Endocrinologist / Geneticist (risk of salt-wasting crisis in infants)
CAH.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24 hours (for newborns/infants); within 1 week (for adults)
CAH.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Classic CAH often involves 'salt-wasting.' Patients may need extra salt in their diet (e.g., adding salt to curd or lassi). Long-term steroid treatment can affect bone density, so ensure adequate calcium from milk, paneer, and ragi. Do not restrict salt unless specifically told by an endocrinologist.
CAH.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: क्लासिक CAH में शरीर से नमक कम हो सकता है। मरीज को आहार में अतिरिक्त नमक की आवश्यकता हो सकती है (जैसे दही या लस्सी में नमक डालना)। हड्डियों की मजबूती के लिए दूध, पनीर और रागी से कैल्शियम लें। डॉक्टर की सलाह के बिना नमक कम न करें।
CAH.source: LOINC Standards (1668-3); Indian Society for Pediatric and Adolescent Endocrinology (ISPAE) guidelines; Newborn Screening India statistics; Labcorp/Metropolis reference intervals. Critical high (>1000 ng/dL) is highly suggestive of 21-hydroxylase deficiency.

T3.note_on_indian_range: Indian reference ranges generally align with international standards (80–200 ng/dL). However, a 2025 multicenter Indian study (N=5,812) highlighted that T3 levels in the Indian population tend to decline modestly with age, especially after 60. Many Indian labs (Metropolis, Dr. Lal PathLabs) emphasize age-specific interpretation. Iodine nutritional status in specific Indian regions (like the sub-Himalayan belt) can also influence baseline T3 levels.
T3.specialist_if_critical_high: Endocrinologist (risk of Thyrotoxicosis or Thyroid Storm)
T3.specialist_if_critical_low: Endocrinologist / Physician (potential Hypothyroidism or Euthyroid Sick Syndrome)
T3.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24 hours (if symptoms of rapid heart rate/fever exist)
T3.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High T3 often indicates hyperthyroidism. Avoid excessive iodine; limit iodized salt, seafood, and kelp. Focus on 'cooling' Indian foods like coconut water (nariyal pani), cucumber, and curd. Consult a specialist for medication rather than self-treating.
T3.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: T3 का उच्च स्तर ओवरएक्टिव थायराइड का संकेत है। ज्यादा आयोडीन से बचें; आयोडीन नमक और समुद्री भोजन कम करें। नारियल पानी, खीरा और दही जैसी ठंडी चीजों का सेवन करें।
T3.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low T3 can signal hypothyroidism or recovery from illness. Ensure use of iodized salt (namak). Include selenium-rich sunflower seeds (surajmukhi), zinc-rich pulses (dal/chana), and dairy (dahi/paneer). Avoid eating raw cruciferous vegetables like cabbage (patta gobhi) in large amounts.
T3.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: T3 का कम स्तर थायराइड की सुस्ती का संकेत हो सकता है। आयोडीन युक्त नमक का प्रयोग करें। सूरजमुखी के बीज, दालें (चना/दाल) और डेयरी उत्पाद (दही/पनीर) लें। कच्ची पत्ता गोभी ज्यादा न खाएं।
T3.source: Mayo Clinic Laboratories (T3 Total Reference); Labcorp Order Code 002188; ResearchGate Indian Multicentre Study (2025/2026); ISPAE Pediatric Thyroid Guidelines. Conversion: ng/dL to nmol/L = x 0.01536.

T4.note_on_indian_range: In India, the reference range for T4 is generally aligned with international standards (approx. 4.5–12.0 mcg/dL). A landmark study by Marwaha et al. (PMID: 23041244) established age-specific thyroid norms for the Indian population, noting that median values can vary slightly due to regional iodine intake. Major Indian diagnostic chains like Dr. Lal PathLabs and Metropolis typically use 4.5–11.5 mcg/dL as the standard adult reference interval.
T4.specialist_if_critical_low: Endocrinologist (risk of Myxedema Coma or severe Hypothyroidism)
T4.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24 hours (if fever, racing heart, or confusion is present)
T4.urgency_if_critical_low: within 24 hours (if extreme lethargy or low body temperature is present)
T4.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High T4 suggests hyperthyroidism. Limit iodine-rich foods like iodized salt and seafood. Incorporate 'cooling' foods such as nariyal pani (coconut water), cucumber (kheera), and seasonal fruits like pineapple or amla. Avoid self-medicating with seaweed or kelp supplements.
T4.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: T4 का उच्च स्तर ओवरएक्टिव थायराइड का संकेत है। ज्यादा आयोडीन वाले भोजन जैसे आयोडीन नमक और समुद्री भोजन सीमित करें। नारियल पानी, खीरा, और ताजे फल (जैसे अनानास) का सेवन करें। बिना डॉक्टर की सलाह के सप्लीमेंट्स न लें।
T4.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low T4 indicates hypothyroidism. Ensure the use of iodized salt (namak). Include selenium and zinc-rich Indian foods like whole pulses (dal/chana), sunflower seeds (surajmukhi), and milk products (paneer/dahi). Cook cruciferous vegetables like cabbage (patta gobhi) or cauliflower (phool gobhi) thoroughly rather than eating them raw.
T4.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: T4 का कम स्तर थायराइड की सुस्ती का संकेत है। आयोडीन युक्त नमक का प्रयोग करें। दालें, चना, सूरजमुखी के बीज और डेयरी उत्पाद (पनीर/दही) लें। पत्ता गोभी या फूल गोभी को हमेशा पकाकर खाएं, कच्चा न लें।
T4.source: LOINC Standard 3026-2; Mayo Clinic Laboratories (T4 Total Reference); Indian Journal of Endocrinology and Metabolism; PMID: 23041244 (Marwaha et al. Indian Adult Thyroid Study). Unit conversion: mcg/dL to nmol/L = x 12.87.

FT3.note_on_indian_range: Indian reference norms established by the Marwaha study (PMID 23041244) involving 1,916 reference adults in Delhi suggest a mean FT3 of 4.6 ± 0.9 pmol/L (approx. 3.0 pg/mL). Indian labs like Metropolis and Dr. Lal PathLabs commonly use 2.0–4.4 pg/mL as the standard adult range. FT3 is notably higher in Indian men compared to women, and levels tend to decline more sharply with age in the Indian population than in Western cohorts.
FT3.specialist_if_critical_high: Endocrinologist (urgent evaluation for T3 Toxicosis or Graves' Disease)
FT3.specialist_if_critical_low: Endocrinologist (investigation for Euthyroid Sick Syndrome or Central Hypothyroidism)
FT3.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High FT3 often signals hyperthyroidism. Avoid iodine-rich foods like iodized salt, seafood, and kelp. Focus on 'cooling' foods such as nariyal pani (coconut water) and curd. Avoid high-dose Biotin supplements for 72 hours before the test as they can cause falsely high results.
FT3.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: FT3 का उच्च स्तर हाइपरथायरायडिज्म का संकेत है। आयोडीन युक्त नमक और समुद्री भोजन से बचें। नारियल पानी और दही जैसे ठंडे खाद्य पदार्थों का सेवन करें। परीक्षण से 72 घंटे पहले बायोटिन (Biotin) सप्लीमेंट न लें।
FT3.source: LOINC Standard 3051-0; Marwaha et al. (2013) Indian Adult Thyroid Study; Metropolis Healthcare Reference Intervals (2025/2026); Quest Diagnostics Methodology (ECLIA).

FT4.note_on_indian_range: The Marwaha Indian adult study (PMID 23041244) reported FT4 ranges of 10.1–24.8 pmol/L (approx. 0.78–1.93 ng/dL). Indian diagnostic standards (e.g., Redcliffe Labs, Apollo Diagnostics) generally use 0.8–1.8 ng/dL. In the Indian context, monitoring FT4 is considered more reliable than Total T4 because it is not affected by protein-binding variations common in Indian dietary profiles or pregnancy.
FT4.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24 hours (emergency if palpitations or fever present)
FT4.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Low FT4 suggests hypothyroidism. Ensure use of iodized salt (namak). Include selenium-rich foods like sunflower seeds (surajmukhi) and zinc-rich pulses (dal). Always cook goitrogenic vegetables like cabbage (patta gobhi) and cauliflower (phool gobhi) thoroughly.
FT4.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: FT4 का कम स्तर हाइपोथायरायडिज्म का संकेत है। आयोडीन युक्त नमक का प्रयोग करें। सूरजमुखी के बीज और दालें लें। पत्ता गोभी और फूल गोभी को हमेशा अच्छी तरह पकाकर खाएं।
FT4.source: LOINC Standard 8478-0; Mayo Clinic Laboratories Reference Values; Indian Journal of Endocrinology and Metabolism; Marwaha et al. (PMID 23041244). Unit conversion: pmol/L to ng/dL = x 0.0777.

Anti-TPO.note_on_indian_range: In India, the reference cutoff is typically <34 or <35 IU/mL. Studies in the Indian population (e.g., Central Kerala study and Marwaha et al.) show a high prevalence of TPO positivity, ranging from 23% to 27% in reproductive-age women. Indian clinical guidelines emphasize that while Western labs often use a stricter <9 IU/mL cutoff, levels between 35–100 IU/mL in the Indian context are often considered 'mildly elevated' and may not require immediate treatment if TSH is normal.
Anti-TPO.specialist_if_critical_high: Endocrinologist (investigation for Hashimoto’s Thyroiditis or Graves’ Disease)
Anti-TPO.urgency_if_critical_high: within 1 week (if accompanied by TSH/T4 abnormalities)
Anti-TPO.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High Anti-TPO indicates an immune attack on the thyroid. Focus on anti-inflammatory Indian foods like turmeric (Haldi), ginger, and garlic. Ensure adequate Selenium from sunflower seeds (Surajmukhi) and Zinc from dals. Many patients find relief by reducing gluten (Wheat/Maida) and dairy, as these can sometimes trigger inflammation in Hashimoto's.
Anti-TPO.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: Anti-TPO का बढ़ा हुआ स्तर थायराइड पर इम्यून सिस्टम के हमले का संकेत है। सूजन कम करने के लिए हल्दी, अदरक और लहसुन का सेवन करें। सेलेनियम के लिए सूरजमुखी के बीज और जिंक के लिए दालें लें। गेहूं (ग्लूटेन) और मैदा कम करने से कई मरीजों को फायदा होता है।
Anti-TPO.source: Apollo 247 Reference Range (<35 IU/mL); Marwaha et al. (PMID 23041244) Indian Thyroid Study; Cleveland Clinic Clinical Guidance (<5.6 IU/mL variation); Jebmh India Research on TPO Prevalence. Note: Levels >1000 IU/mL strongly suggest active autoimmune thyroiditis.
